Galaxy3D Engine — Public API Reference

Galaxy3D Engine — Référence de l'API publique

This document lists every public symbol exported by both crates of the engine. Use the sidebar on the left to navigate by namespace, file and element. Each entry shows its Rust signature, a description (English / French), and a small usage example. Switch language at any time with the EN / FR buttons; toggle theme with the ☾ / ☀ button.

Ce document liste chaque symbole public exporté par les deux crates du moteur. Utilisez la barre latérale à gauche pour naviguer par namespace, fichier et élément. Chaque entrée présente sa signature Rust, une description (anglais / français), et un petit exemple d'utilisation. Changez de langue à tout moment avec les boutons EN / FR ; basculez le thème avec le bouton ☾ / ☀.

Crate map

Carte des crates

  • galaxy_3d_engine — core engine: types, scene, render graph, resource manager.moteur principal : types, scène, render graph, gestionnaire de ressources.
  • galaxy_3d_engine_renderer_vulkan — Vulkan backend implementing every graphics_device trait.backend Vulkan implémentant chaque trait graphics_device.

galaxy_3d_engine::camera::camera

galaxy_3d_engine::camera::camera

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/camera/camera.rs
struct

Camera

pub struct Camera

The CPU-side camera. Stores its view matrix, projection matrix, derived frustum, viewport and optional scissor. Passive container — does no math beyond view_projection_matrix() = projection * view.

Build it via Camera::new(view, projection, frustum, viewport); mutate via set_view, set_projection, set_frustum, set_viewport, set_scissor. Used as a snapshot by the culler and view dispatcher.

La caméra côté CPU. Stocke sa matrice de view, sa matrice de projection, le frustum dérivé, son viewport et un scissor optionnel. Conteneur passif — ne fait aucun calcul autre que view_projection_matrix() = projection * view.

Construite via Camera::new(view, projection, frustum, viewport) ; mutée via set_view, set_projection, set_frustum, set_viewport, set_scissor. Utilisée comme instantané par le culler et le view dispatcher.

Example

Exemple

let view = Mat4::look_at_rh(eye, target, Vec3::Y);
let proj = Mat4::perspective_rh(60_f32.to_radians(), aspect, 0.1, 1000.0);
let vp   = proj * view;
let frustum = Frustum::from_view_projection(&vp);
let camera = Camera::new(view, proj, frustum, Viewport {
    x: 0.0, y: 0.0, width: 1920.0, height: 1080.0, min_depth: 0.0, max_depth: 1.0,
});
method

Camera::effective_scissor

pub fn effective_scissor(&self) -> Rect2D
Effective scissor: explicit scissor or viewport bounds as Rect2D.
Effective scissor: explicit scissor or viewport bounds as Rect2D.

The scissor defaults to None (same as viewport).
Create a new camera with the given parameters.
The scissor defaults to None (same as viewport).

galaxy_3d_engine::camera::frustum

galaxy_3d_engine::camera::frustum

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/camera/frustum.rs
struct

Frustum

pub struct Frustum

Six-plane view frustum derived from a view-projection matrix via the Gribb–Hartmann extraction. Each plane is stored as (A, B, C, D) with the normal pointing inward; a point P is inside if dot(plane.xyz, P) + plane.w >= 0. Works identically for perspective and orthographic projections.

Frustum de vue à six plans dérivé d'une matrice view-projection via l'extraction de Gribb–Hartmann. Chaque plan est stocké sous forme (A, B, C, D) avec la normale pointant vers l'intérieur ; un point P est à l'intérieur si dot(plane.xyz, P) + plane.w >= 0. Fonctionne identiquement pour les projections perspective et orthographique.

Example

Exemple

let frustum = Frustum::from_view_projection(&view_projection);
if frustum.intersects_aabb(&world_aabb) {
    // visible — render
}
field

Frustum::planes

pub planes: [Vec4; 6]
Frustum planes: left, right, bottom, top, near, far
Frustum planes: left, right, bottom, top, near, far

Example

Exemple

let value = instance.planes;
method

Frustum::classify_aabb

pub fn classify_aabb(&self, aabb: &AABB) -> FrustumTest

Three-way classification: Inside, Outside, Partial. Used by hierarchical cullers (octree) to skip an entire subtree when fully inside (collect everything below without further testing) or fully outside (skip).

Classification à trois états : Inside, Outside, Partial. Utilisé par les cullers hiérarchiques (octree) pour sauter un sous-arbre entier quand complètement à l'intérieur (collecter tout sans tester) ou complètement à l'extérieur (sauter).

Example

Exemple

match frustum.classify_aabb(&node.aabb) {
    FrustumClassify::Inside  => collect_all_recursive(node),
    FrustumClassify::Outside => /* skip */ {}
    FrustumClassify::Partial => recurse(node),
}
method

Frustum::from_view_projection

pub fn from_view_projection(vp: &Mat4) -> Self
Extract frustum planes from a view-projection matrix.
Uses the Gribb & Hartmann method. Works for both perspective and orthographic projections.
Extract frustum planes from a view-projection matrix.
Uses the Gribb & Hartmann method. Works for both perspective and orthographic projections.

Example

Exemple

let result = Frustum::from_view_projection(/* args */);
method

Frustum::intersects_aabb

pub fn intersects_aabb(&self, aabb: &AABB) -> bool

Test whether an AABB intersects this frustum (positive-vertex test). Conservative: may report a false positive for AABBs that diagonally clip a corner, but never a false negative. Returns true if the AABB is fully or partially inside, false only if every corner is on the outer side of at least one plane.

Teste si une AABB intersecte ce frustum (test du sommet positif). Conservateur : peut signaler un faux positif pour des AABB qui dépassent diagonalement un coin, mais jamais un faux négatif. Retourne true si l'AABB est entièrement ou partiellement dedans, false uniquement si chaque coin est du mauvais côté d'au moins un plan.

Example

Exemple

if frustum.intersects_aabb(&aabb) { /* render */ }
enum

FrustumTest

pub enum FrustumTest
Result of a 3-way frustum/AABB classification.
Used by spatial acceleration structures (OctreeSceneIndex) for efficient hierarchical culling:
  • Outside → skip the entire subtree
  • Inside → collect all objects without further testing
  • Partial → test individual objects and recurse into children
Result of a 3-way frustum/AABB classification.
Used by spatial acceleration structures (OctreeSceneIndex) for efficient hierarchical culling:
  • Outside → skip the entire subtree
  • Inside → collect all objects without further testing
  • Partial → test individual objects and recurse into children

Example

Exemple

// Match on variants:
match value { FrustumTest::Foo => /* ... */, _ => /* ... */ }
variant

FrustumTest::Inside

Inside
AABB is entirely inside the frustum
AABB is entirely inside the frustum

Example

Exemple

let v = FrustumTest::Inside;
variant

FrustumTest::Outside

Outside
AABB is entirely outside the frustum
AABB is entirely outside the frustum

Example

Exemple

let v = FrustumTest::Outside;
variant

FrustumTest::Partial

Partial
AABB partially overlaps the frustum
AABB partially overlaps the frustum

Example

Exemple

let v = FrustumTest::Partial;

galaxy_3d_engine::camera::lod

galaxy_3d_engine::camera::lod

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/camera/lod.rs
fn

project_sphere_diameter

pub fn project_sphere_diameter( center_ws: Vec3, radius_ws: f32, camera: &Camera, ) -> f32

Project a bounding sphere onto the camera's screen and return its diameter in pixels. Used to drive screen-space LOD selection: feed the result to scene::lod::apply_hysteresis together with the LOD thresholds to pick a stable LOD.

Returns a saturated large value when the sphere is behind the camera (so the most-detailed LOD is picked safely; downstream culling drops it).

Projette une sphère englobante sur l'écran de la caméra et retourne son diamètre en pixels. Utilisé pour piloter la sélection de LOD en espace écran : passez le résultat à scene::lod::apply_hysteresis avec les seuils de LOD pour choisir un LOD stable.

Retourne une grande valeur saturée quand la sphère est derrière la caméra (afin que le LOD le plus détaillé soit choisi sans risque ; le culling en aval éliminera l'objet).

Example

Exemple

let center = (aabb.min + aabb.max) * 0.5;
let radius = (aabb.max - aabb.min).length() * 0.5;
let screen_diameter = project_sphere_diameter(center, radius, &camera);
let new_lod = apply_hysteresis(current_lod, screen_diameter, &thresholds);

galaxy_3d_engine::camera::visible_instances

galaxy_3d_engine::camera::visible_instances

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/camera/visible_instances.rs
struct

VisibleInstance

pub struct VisibleInstance
A single visible instance with its view-space depth.
distance is the raw view-space depth in world units (positive in front of the camera, negative behind). It is intentionally kept as a f32 so that multiple consumers — sort key encoding, LOD selection, distance fade, etc. — can interpret it as needed without precision loss.
A single visible instance with its view-space depth.
distance is the raw view-space depth in world units (positive in front of the camera, negative behind). It is intentionally kept as a f32 so that multiple consumers — sort key encoding, LOD selection, distance fade, etc. — can interpret it as needed without precision loss.

Example

Exemple

// Use the type in a function signature or storage:
let x: VisibleInstance;
field

VisibleInstance::distance

pub distance: f32
Public field of VisibleInstance.
Champ public de VisibleInstance.

Example

Exemple

let value = instance.distance;
field

VisibleInstance::key

pub key: RenderInstanceKey
Public field of VisibleInstance.
Champ public de VisibleInstance.

Example

Exemple

let value = instance.key;
struct

VisibleInstances

pub struct VisibleInstances

The output buffer of culling and the input of view dispatch. Holds a snapshot of the camera plus a Vec<VisibleInstance> with each visible RenderInstanceKey and its view-space depth. Reused across frames via set_camera + clear_instances (capacity preserved — zero allocation in steady state).

Buffer de sortie du culling et d'entrée du view dispatch. Contient un instantané de la caméra et un Vec<VisibleInstance> avec chaque RenderInstanceKey visible et sa profondeur en espace view. Réutilisé entre frames via set_camera + clear_instances (capacité préservée — zéro allocation en régime stationnaire).

Example

Exemple

let mut visible = VisibleInstances::new_empty();
frustum_culler.cull_into(&scene, &camera, Some(&octree), &mut visible);
for v in visible.instances() {
    println!("{:?} at depth {}", v.key, v.distance);
}
method

VisibleInstances::camera

pub fn camera(&self) -> &Camera
Camera snapshot at the time of culling.
Camera snapshot at the time of culling.

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.camera(/* args */);
method

pub fn clear_instances(&mut self)
Clear all instances (preserves capacity for reuse).
Clear all instances (preserves capacity for reuse).

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.clear_instances(/* args */);

Read-only slice of all visible instances.
Read-only slice of all visible instances.

Used by CameraCuller::cull_into() and SceneIndex::query_frustum() to push visible instances.

Used by CameraCuller::cull_into() and SceneIndex::query_frustum() to push visible instances.

Whether the list is empty.

Example

Exemple

method

VisibleInstances::new_empty

pub fn new_empty() -> Self
Create an empty VisibleInstances with a placeholder camera.
The placeholder camera will be overwritten on the first call to CameraCuller::cull_into(). The internal buffer starts with zero capacity and grows on first cull; subsequent culls reuse the existing capacity.
Create an empty VisibleInstances with a placeholder camera.
The placeholder camera will be overwritten on the first call to CameraCuller::cull_into(). The internal buffer starts with zero capacity and grows on first cull; subsequent culls reuse the existing capacity.

Example

Exemple

let result = VisibleInstances::new_empty(/* args */);
method

Replace the camera snapshot. Called by CameraCuller::cull_into().
Replace the camera snapshot. Called by CameraCuller::cull_into().

galaxy_3d_engine::engine

galaxy_3d_engine::engine

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/engine.rs
struct

Engine

pub struct Engine

The Engine singleton is the entry-point of the entire Galaxy3D rendering library. It is a zero-sized type whose static methods own and gate access to four global subsystems: the graphics device map, the ResourceManager, the SceneManager, and the RenderGraphManager, plus the global Logger.

Lifecycle is strict: call Engine::initialize() once at application start; create the graphics device, resource manager, scene manager, and render-graph manager via the corresponding create_* functions; then run frames; finally call Engine::shutdown() at exit to drop everything in the right order. Subsequent initialize() calls are idempotent (the OnceLock backs the global state).

All getters return Arc<Mutex<T>> handles which can be locked from any thread — but in practice the engine is single-threaded by contract: command-list recording, scene mutations and submission happen sequentially.

Le singleton Engine est le point d'entrée de toute la bibliothèque de rendu Galaxy3D. C'est un type de taille nulle dont les méthodes statiques possèdent et contrôlent l'accès à quatre sous-systèmes globaux : la carte des graphics devices, le ResourceManager, le SceneManager, et le RenderGraphManager, ainsi que le Logger global.

Le cycle de vie est strict : appelez Engine::initialize() une fois au démarrage de l'application ; créez le graphics device, le resource manager, le scene manager et le render-graph manager via les fonctions create_* correspondantes ; lancez ensuite vos frames ; enfin appelez Engine::shutdown() à la sortie pour libérer le tout dans le bon ordre. Les appels suivants à initialize() sont idempotents (le OnceLock sous-jacent garantit l'état global).

Tous les getters retournent des handles Arc<Mutex<T>> qui peuvent être verrouillés depuis n'importe quel thread — mais en pratique le moteur est single-thread par contrat : l'enregistrement des command-lists, les mutations de la scène et la soumission se font séquentiellement.

Example

Exemple

use galaxy_3d_engine::galaxy3d::Engine;
use galaxy_3d_engine_renderer_vulkan::create_vulkan_graphics_device;

Engine::initialize()?;
let gd = Engine::create_graphics_device("main", create_vulkan_graphics_device(&window, Default::default())?)?;
Engine::create_resource_manager()?;
Engine::create_scene_manager()?;
Engine::create_render_graph_manager()?;
// ... main loop ...
Engine::shutdown();
method

Engine::create_graphics_device

pub fn create_graphics_device<R: GraphicsDevice + 'static>(name: &str, graphics_device: R) -> Result<Arc<Mutex<dyn GraphicsDevice>>>

Wrap a backend graphics device in Arc<Mutex<dyn GraphicsDevice>>, register it under name, and return the wrapped handle for immediate use. Multiple named devices can coexist; the engine code consistently uses "main" as the canonical name.

Errors:

  • InitializationFailed if the engine isn't initialized.
  • InitializationFailed if a device with the same name already exists — call destroy_graphics_device(name) first.
  • BackendError if the internal lock is poisoned.

Encapsule un graphics device backend dans Arc<Mutex<dyn GraphicsDevice>>, l'enregistre sous le nom name et retourne le handle enveloppé pour usage immédiat. Plusieurs devices nommés peuvent coexister ; le code du moteur utilise systématiquement "main" comme nom canonique.

Erreurs :

  • InitializationFailed si le moteur n'est pas initialisé.
  • InitializationFailed si un device du même nom existe déjà — appelez d'abord destroy_graphics_device(name).
  • BackendError si le verrou interne est empoisonné.

Example

Exemple

let gd = Engine::create_graphics_device(
    "main",
    create_vulkan_graphics_device(&window, Default::default())?,
)?;
method

Engine::create_render_graph_manager

pub fn create_render_graph_manager() -> Result<()>

Create the global RenderGraphManager singleton. The manager owns every RenderGraph, RenderPass, GraphResource and Framebuffer in the application. Call once after create_graphics_device and create_resource_manager — both must be ready because the manager will allocate backend objects (render passes, framebuffers) at pass-creation time.

Crée le singleton global RenderGraphManager. Le manager possède chaque RenderGraph, RenderPass, GraphResource et Framebuffer de l'application. À appeler une fois après create_graphics_device et create_resource_manager — les deux doivent être prêts car le manager allouera des objets backend (render passes, framebuffers) lors de la création des passes.

Example

Exemple

Engine::create_render_graph_manager()?;
let rgm_arc = Engine::render_graph_manager()?;
let mut rgm = rgm_arc.lock().unwrap();
let graph = rgm.create_render_graph("main", 2 /* frames in flight */)?;
method

Engine::create_resource_manager

pub fn create_resource_manager() -> Result<()>

Create the global ResourceManager singleton. The manager owns every CPU-side resource (textures, buffers, geometries, materials, meshes, shaders, pipelines) and exposes typed slot-map keys for stable references. Call once after initialize; you can drop and recreate it via destroy_resource_manager() + create_resource_manager() for level transitions.

Crée le singleton global ResourceManager. Le manager possède chaque ressource côté CPU (textures, buffers, géométries, matériaux, meshes, shaders, pipelines) et expose des clés de slot-map typées pour des références stables. À appeler une fois après initialize ; vous pouvez le détruire et le recréer via destroy_resource_manager() + create_resource_manager() pour des transitions de niveau.

Example

Exemple

Engine::create_resource_manager()?;
let rm_arc = Engine::resource_manager()?;
let mut rm = rm_arc.lock().unwrap();
method

Engine::create_scene_manager

pub fn create_scene_manager() -> Result<()>

Create the global SceneManager singleton. Currently the manager wraps a single Scene instance; future versions may expose multi-scene transitions. Call once after create_resource_manager.

Crée le singleton global SceneManager. Actuellement le manager encapsule une seule instance de Scene ; de futures versions pourraient exposer des transitions multi-scènes. À appeler une fois après create_resource_manager.

Example

Exemple

Engine::create_scene_manager()?;
let sm = Engine::scene_manager()?;
method

Engine::destroy_graphics_device

pub fn destroy_graphics_device(name: &str) -> Result<()>

Unregister and drop the named graphics device. Resources you still hold via Arc clones remain valid until you drop them.

Désenregistre et libère le graphics device nommé. Les ressources que vous détenez encore via des clones Arc restent valides jusqu'à ce que vous les libériez.

Example

Exemple

Engine::destroy_graphics_device("main")?;
method

Engine::destroy_render_graph_manager

pub fn destroy_render_graph_manager() -> Result<()>
Destroy the render graph manager singleton
Removes the render graph manager singleton, allowing a new one to be created.

Errors


Returns an error if the engine is not initialized
Destroy the render graph manager singleton
Removes the render graph manager singleton, allowing a new one to be created.

Errors


Returns an error if the engine is not initialized

Example

Exemple

let result = Engine::destroy_render_graph_manager(/* args */)?;
method

pub fn destroy_resource_manager() -> Result<()>
Destroy the resource manager singleton
Removes the resource manager singleton, allowing a new one to be created.

Errors


Returns an error if the engine is not initialized
Destroy the resource manager singleton
Removes the resource manager singleton, allowing a new one to be created.

Errors


Returns an error if the engine is not initialized

Example

Exemple

let result = Engine::destroy_resource_manager(/* args */)?;
method

Engine::destroy_scene_manager

pub fn destroy_scene_manager() -> Result<()>
Destroy the scene manager singleton
Removes the scene manager singleton, allowing a new one to be created.

Errors


Returns an error if the engine is not initialized
Destroy the scene manager singleton
Removes the scene manager singleton, allowing a new one to be created.

Errors


Returns an error if the engine is not initialized

Example

Exemple

method

Engine::graphics_device

pub fn graphics_device(name: &str) -> Result<Arc<Mutex<dyn GraphicsDevice>>>

Look up a graphics device by name and return a clone of its Arc<Mutex<dyn GraphicsDevice>> handle. The returned handle can be locked from any thread but the device itself is single-threaded internally.

Recherche un graphics device par son nom et retourne un clone de son handle Arc<Mutex<dyn GraphicsDevice>>. Le handle retourné peut être verrouillé depuis n'importe quel thread mais le device lui-même est single-thread en interne.

Example

Exemple

let gd_arc = Engine::graphics_device("main")?;
let gd = gd_arc.lock().unwrap();
// use the device for command-list creation, submission, etc.
method

Engine::graphics_device_count

pub fn graphics_device_count() -> usize
Get the number of registered graphics devices
Get the number of registered graphics devices

Example

Exemple

let result = Engine::graphics_device_count(/* args */);
method

Engine::graphics_device_names

pub fn graphics_device_names() -> Vec<String>
Get the names of all registered graphics devices
Get the names of all registered graphics devices

Example

Exemple

let result = Engine::graphics_device_names(/* args */);
method

Engine::initialize

pub fn initialize() -> Result<()>

Initialize the engine. Must be called once at application startup before creating any subsystem. Subsequent calls are idempotent and always succeed (the underlying OnceLock only fires the initializer the first time).

Returns a Result for future extensibility (currently always Ok). Pair every initialize() with a matching shutdown() at application exit.

Initialise le moteur. Doit être appelé une seule fois au démarrage de l'application avant de créer le moindre sous-système. Les appels suivants sont idempotents et réussissent toujours (le OnceLock sous-jacent ne déclenche l'initialisation qu'à la première invocation).

Retourne un Result pour permettre une extension future (toujours Ok actuellement). Associez chaque initialize() à un shutdown() correspondant à la fin de l'application.

Example

Exemple

Engine::initialize()?;
method

Engine::log

pub fn log(severity: LogSeverity, source: &str, message: String)
Internal logging method (for simple logs without file:line)
Used by macros like engine_info!, engine_warn!, etc.

Arguments


  • severity - Log severity level
  • source - Source module (e.g., "galaxy3d::Engine")
  • message - Log message
Internal logging method (for simple logs without file:line)
Used by macros like engine_info!, engine_warn!, etc.

Arguments


  • severity - Log severity level
  • source - Source module (e.g., "galaxy3d::Engine")
  • message - Log message

Example

Exemple

method

Engine::log_detailed

pub fn log_detailed( severity: LogSeverity, source: &str, message: String, file: &'static str, line: u32, )
Internal logging method with file:line information (for ERROR logs)
Used by engine_error! macro to include source location.

Arguments


  • severity - Log severity level (typically Error)
  • source - Source module (e.g., "galaxy3d::Engine")
  • message - Log message
  • file - Source file path
  • line - Source line number
Internal logging method with file:line information (for ERROR logs)
Used by engine_error! macro to include source location.

Arguments


  • severity - Log severity level (typically Error)
  • source - Source module (e.g., "galaxy3d::Engine")
  • message - Log message
  • file - Source file path
  • line - Source line number

Example

Exemple

let result = Engine::log_detailed(/* args */);
method

Engine::render_graph_manager

pub fn render_graph_manager() -> Result<Arc<Mutex<RenderGraphManager>>>
Get the render graph manager singleton
Provides global access to the render graph manager after it has been created.

Returns


A shared pointer to the RenderGraphManager wrapped in a Mutex for thread-safe access

Errors


Returns an error if:
  • The engine is not initialized
  • The render graph manager has not been created
Get the render graph manager singleton
Provides global access to the render graph manager after it has been created.

Returns


A shared pointer to the RenderGraphManager wrapped in a Mutex for thread-safe access

Errors

  • The engine is not initialized
  • The render graph manager has not been created

Engine::resource_manager

pub fn resource_manager() -> Result<Arc<Mutex<ResourceManager>>>

Get the global ResourceManager Arc handle. Lock to mutate or query the resource registry. The lock is short-lived in well-behaved code; the engine's own systems (drawer, render graph) acquire and release it explicitly.

Récupère le handle Arc global du ResourceManager. Verrouillez-le pour muter ou interroger le registre de ressources. Le verrou doit être court dans un code correct ; les systèmes du moteur (drawer, render graph) l'acquièrent et le libèrent explicitement.

Example

Exemple

let rm_arc = Engine::resource_manager()?;
let rm = rm_arc.lock().unwrap();
let mesh = rm.mesh_by_name("player");
method

Engine::scene_manager

pub fn scene_manager() -> Result<Arc<Mutex<SceneManager>>>
Get the scene manager singleton
Provides global access to the scene manager after it has been created.

Returns


A shared pointer to the SceneManager wrapped in a Mutex for thread-safe access

Errors


Returns an error if:
  • The engine is not initialized
  • The scene manager has not been created
Get the scene manager singleton
Provides global access to the scene manager after it has been created.

Returns


A shared pointer to the SceneManager wrapped in a Mutex for thread-safe access

Errors


Returns an error if:
  • The engine is not initialized
  • The scene manager has not been created

Example

Exemple

let result = Engine::scene_manager(/* args */)?;
method

Engine::set_logger

pub fn set_logger<L: Logger + 'static>(logger: L)

Replace the global logger with a custom implementation (file logger, network logger, in-test capturing logger, …). Any type implementing Logger is accepted; the previous logger is dropped.

Remplace le logger global par une implémentation personnalisée (logger fichier, logger réseau, logger de capture pour les tests…). Tout type implémentant Logger est accepté ; le logger précédent est libéré.

Example

Exemple

struct FileLogger { /* ... */ }
impl Logger for FileLogger { /* ... */ }
Engine::set_logger(FileLogger::open("engine.log")?);
method

Engine::shutdown

pub fn shutdown()

Shut the engine down and drop every subsystem in reverse dependency order: render-graph manager → scene manager → resource manager → graphics devices. Defensive against poisoned RwLocks — never panics.

After shutdown, you must call initialize() again before creating new subsystems. Resources still held by your code (Arc clones) remain valid; only the engine's references are dropped.

Arrête le moteur et libère chaque sous-système dans l'ordre inverse des dépendances : render-graph manager → scene manager → resource manager → graphics devices. Robuste face aux RwLock empoisonnés — ne panique jamais.

Après shutdown, vous devez appeler initialize() à nouveau avant de créer de nouveaux sous-systèmes. Les ressources encore détenues par votre code (clones d'Arc) restent valides ; seules les références du moteur sont libérées.

Example

Exemple

Engine::shutdown();

galaxy_3d_engine::error

galaxy_3d_engine::error

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/error.rs
enum

Error

pub enum Error

The unified error type returned by every engine API. Four variants:

  • BackendError(String) — the catch-all for rendering or device failures: lock poisoning, plugin call failures, every engine_bail! path.
  • OutOfMemory — GPU allocation exhausted (reserved; not currently produced by the Vulkan backend, which surfaces alloc failures via BackendError).
  • InvalidResource(String) — resource creation rejected by validation (e.g. mismatched mesh submeshes, illegal atlas region).
  • InitializationFailed(String) — engine not initialized, manager already / not created, plugin not found.

All variants implement Display with a clear message; the engine's macro family (engine_bail!, engine_err!) consistently constructs BackendError while logging.

Le type d'erreur unifié retourné par chaque API du moteur. Quatre variantes :

  • BackendError(String) — le fourre-tout pour les défaillances de rendu ou de device : empoisonnement de verrou, échec d'appel de plugin, tout chemin engine_bail!.
  • OutOfMemory — mémoire GPU épuisée (réservé ; pas produit actuellement par le backend Vulkan, qui remonte les échecs d'allocation via BackendError).
  • InvalidResource(String) — création de ressource rejetée par la validation (par exemple submeshes incompatibles, région d'atlas illégale).
  • InitializationFailed(String) — moteur non initialisé, manager déjà ou pas encore créé, plugin introuvable.

Toutes les variantes implémentent Display avec un message clair ; la famille de macros du moteur (engine_bail!, engine_err!) construit systématiquement BackendError en loggant.

Example

Exemple

match Engine::resource_manager() {
    Ok(rm) => { /* ... */ }
    Err(Error::InitializationFailed(msg)) => eprintln!("setup error: {}", msg),
    Err(e) => return Err(e),
}
variant

Error::BackendError

BackendError(String)
Backend-specific error (Vulkan, DirectX, etc.)
Backend-specific error (Vulkan, DirectX, etc.)

Example

Exemple

let v = Error::BackendError;
variant

Error::InitializationFailed

InitializationFailed(String)
Initialization failed (engine, graphics_device, subsystems)
Initialization failed (engine, graphics_device, subsystems)

Example

Exemple

let v = Error::InitializationFailed;
variant

Error::InvalidResource

InvalidResource(String)
Invalid resource (texture, buffer, shader, etc.)
Invalid resource (texture, buffer, shader, etc.)

Example

Exemple

let v = Error::InvalidResource;
variant

Error::OutOfMemory

OutOfMemory
Out of GPU memory
Out of GPU memory

Example

Exemple

let v = Error::OutOfMemory;
type

Result

pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, Error>

The crate-wide Result alias: std::result::Result<T, Error>. Use it everywhere you call into the engine.

L'alias Result applicable à tout le crate : std::result::Result<T, Error>. Utilisez-le partout où vous appelez le moteur.

Example

Exemple

fn setup() -> galaxy_3d_engine::galaxy3d::Result<()> {
    Engine::initialize()?;
    Ok(())
}

galaxy_3d_engine::graphics_device::access_type

galaxy_3d_engine::graphics_device::access_type

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/graphics_device/access_type.rs
struct

BufferAccess

pub struct BufferAccess
Per-buffer access declaration for a render pass.
Counterpart to ImageAccess for SSBO/UBO/vertex/index buffers that need an execution + memory barrier between passes (e.g. a compute pass writing a buffer that a fragment pass reads).
Passed to begin_render_pass() alongside ImageAccesses — the backend merges them into a single vkCmdPipelineBarrier2.
Per-buffer access declaration for a render pass.
Counterpart to ImageAccess for SSBO/UBO/vertex/index buffers that need an execution + memory barrier between passes (e.g. a compute pass writing a buffer that a fragment pass reads).
Passed to begin_render_pass() alongside ImageAccesses — the backend merges them into a single vkCmdPipelineBarrier2.

Example

Exemple

// Use the type in a function signature or storage:
let x: BufferAccess;

How this buffer is accessed in the pass
How this buffer is accessed in the pass

The GPU buffer being accessed

BufferAccess::previous_access_type

How this buffer was accessed previously (None = first use)
How this buffer was accessed previously (None = first use)

struct

ImageAccess

pub struct ImageAccess
Per-image access declaration for a render pass.
Passed to begin_render_pass() so the backend can emit layout transitions and memory barriers internally.
previous_access_type is precalculated by the render graph during compile() — the backend uses it to determine the source layout and pipeline stage for barrier emission.
Per-image access declaration for a render pass.
Passed to begin_render_pass() so the backend can emit layout transitions and memory barriers internally.
previous_access_type is precalculated by the render graph during compile() — the backend uses it to determine the source layout and pipeline stage for barrier emission.

Example

Exemple

// Use the type in a function signature or storage:
let x: ImageAccess;

enum

AccessType

pub enum AccessType
How a pass accesses a resource.
Semantic descriptor — the backend maps this to the appropriate pipeline stages, access masks, and image layouts internally. Inspired by Frostbite Frame Graph, Unreal Engine 5 RDG, and Granite.
How a pass accesses a resource.
Semantic descriptor — the backend maps this to the appropriate pipeline stages, access masks, and image layouts internally. Inspired by Frostbite Frame Graph, Unreal Engine 5 RDG, and Granite.

Example

Exemple

// Match on variants:
match value { AccessType::Foo => /* ... */, _ => /* ... */ }

AccessType::is_attachment

pub fn is_attachment(self) -> bool
Returns true if this access type is a color or depth/stencil attachment (i.e. participates in a render pass as an attachment).
Returns true if this access type is a color or depth/stencil attachment (i.e. participates in a render pass as an attachment).

BindingGroupLayoutDesc

pub struct BindingGroupLayoutDesc
Description of a BindingGroup layout (blueprint for a set of bindings)
This replaces raw descriptor set layout handles in the abstract graphics_device layer. The backend creates the actual GPU layout object from this description.

trait

BindingGroup

pub trait BindingGroup: Send + Sync
An immutable set of GPU resource bindings.
The layout and pool are managed internally by the graphics_device. Once created, a BindingGroup cannot be modified — create a new one to change resources.
An immutable set of GPU resource bindings.
The layout and pool are managed internally by the graphics_device. Once created, a BindingGroup cannot be modified — create a new one to change resources.

galaxy_3d_engine::graphics_device::frame_buffer

galaxy_3d_engine::graphics_device::frame_buffer

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/graphics_device/frame_buffer.rs
struct

FramebufferAttachment

pub struct FramebufferAttachment
A single framebuffer attachment: a texture + a mip level + a layer range.
Maps directly to a VkImageView in the backend, with level_count = 1 (Vulkan does not allow rendering into multiple mip levels of an attachment simultaneously) and a configurable layer_count for layered rendering (cubemap one-pass, cascaded shadow maps, multiview/VR, voxelization).
A single framebuffer attachment: a texture + a mip level + a layer range.
Maps directly to a VkImageView in the backend, with level_count = 1 (Vulkan does not allow rendering into multiple mip levels of an attachment simultaneously) and a configurable layer_count for layered rendering (cubemap one-pass, cascaded shadow maps, multiview/VR, voxelization).

Example

Exemple

// Use the type in a function signature or storage:
let x: FramebufferAttachment;
field

FramebufferAttachment::base_array_layer

First array layer to render into (0 = base layer).

Mip level to render into (0 = base level).
Mip level to render into (0 = base level).

Example

Exemple

let value = instance.base_mip_level;
field

FramebufferAttachment::layer_count

pub layer_count: u32
Number of consecutive layers to render into. Must be >= 1. Use > 1 only for layered rendering (e.g. cubemap one-pass with 6).
Number of consecutive layers to render into. Must be >= 1. Use > 1 only for layered rendering (e.g. cubemap one-pass with 6).

BindlessConfig

pub struct BindlessConfig

Hard caps on the number of textures of each type the bindless descriptor set can hold. Fixed at initialization — the Vulkan descriptor set is allocated with these sizes, so changing them requires reinitializing the device. Defaults: 4096 / 64 / 16 / 64 (2D / cube / 3D / 2D-array).

Plafonds sur le nombre de textures de chaque type que le descriptor set bindless peut contenir. Fixés à l'initialisation — le descriptor set Vulkan est alloué avec ces tailles, donc les changer nécessite de réinitialiser le device. Valeurs par défaut : 4096 / 64 / 16 / 64 (2D / cube / 3D / 2D-array).

Example

Exemple

// Increase the 2D limit to 8192 for a texture-heavy game:
let bindless = BindlessConfig {
    max_texture_2d: 8192,
    ..Default::default()
};
field

BindlessConfig::max_texture_2d

pub max_texture_2d: u32
Maximum number of 2D textures (default: 4096)
Maximum number of 2D textures (default: 4096)

struct

Config

pub struct Config

Configuration passed to the backend at startup. Controls validation layers, debug routing, application metadata, panic-on-error, and the bindless table sizes (BindlessConfig). Defaults are sensible: validation enabled in debug builds, ErrorsAndWarnings severity, console output, all message categories on.

Configuration transmise au backend au démarrage. Contrôle les couches de validation, le routage du debug, les métadonnées de l'application, l'option panic-on-error, et la taille des tables bindless (BindlessConfig). Les valeurs par défaut sont sensées : validation active en build debug, sévérité ErrorsAndWarnings, sortie console, toutes les catégories de messages activées.

Example

Exemple

let config = Config {
    app_name: "My Game".to_string(),
    enable_validation: cfg!(debug_assertions),
    bindless: BindlessConfig { max_texture_2d: 8192, ..Default::default() },
    ..Default::default()
};
field

GraphicsDevicePluginRegistry::create_graphics_device

pub fn create_graphics_device(&self, plugin_name: &str, window: &Window, config: Config) -> Result<Arc<Mutex<dyn GraphicsDevice>>>
Create a graphics device using a registered plugin

Arguments


  • plugin_name - Name of the plugin to use
  • window - Window to render to
  • config - Graphics device configuration

Returns


A shared, thread-safe graphics device instance

Returns

GraphicsDeviceStats

pub struct GraphicsDeviceStats

Per-frame statistics returned by GraphicsDevice::stats(): draw calls issued, triangles produced, total GPU memory used (allocator high-water mark in bytes).

Statistiques par frame retournées par GraphicsDevice::stats() : nombre de draw calls émis, nombre de triangles produits, mémoire GPU totale utilisée (high-water mark de l'allocateur en octets).

Example

Exemple

let s = gd.stats();
egui::Window::new("GPU").show(&ctx, |ui| {
    ui.label(format!("{} draws / {:.1} M tris", s.draw_calls, s.triangles as f32 / 1e6));
});

trait

GraphicsDevice

pub trait GraphicsDevice: Send + Sync

The single trait that every backend (Vulkan, future DX12 / Metal) must implement. It is the entire backend-facing surface of the engine: resource creation, command-list creation, binding-group creation, submission and synchronization, plus per-frame statistics and resize notifications.

Backends register themselves via register_graphics_device_plugin("name", factory), then the application picks one by name. The engine itself never depends on a specific backend at compile time.

Implementations are Send + Sync; the engine wraps each instance in Arc<Mutex<_>> so any thread can lock the device, but command recording must happen serially within one thread.

Le trait unique que chaque backend (Vulkan, DX12 ou Metal à l'avenir) doit implémenter. C'est toute la surface backend du moteur : création de ressources, création de command-lists, création de binding-groups, soumission et synchronisation, plus les statistiques par frame et les notifications de resize.

Les backends s'enregistrent via register_graphics_device_plugin("nom", factory), puis l'application choisit l'un d'eux par son nom. Le moteur lui-même ne dépend d'aucun backend spécifique à la compilation.

Les implémentations sont Send + Sync ; le moteur encapsule chaque instance dans Arc<Mutex<_>> donc n'importe quel thread peut verrouiller le device, mais l'enregistrement de commandes doit rester sériel au sein d'un même thread.

Example

Exemple

let gd_arc = Engine::graphics_device("main")?;
let mut gd = gd_arc.lock().unwrap();
let buffer = gd.create_buffer(BufferDesc { size: 4096, usage: BufferUsage::Uniform })?;
fn

graphics_device_plugin_registry

pub fn graphics_device_plugin_registry() -> &'static Mutex<Option<GraphicsDevicePluginRegistry>>
Get the global graphics device plugin registry
Get the global graphics device plugin registry

Example

Exemple

let result = graphics_device_plugin_registry(/* args */);
fn

register_graphics_device_plugin

pub fn register_graphics_device_plugin<F>(name: &'static str, factory: F) where F: Fn(&Window, Config) -> Result<Arc<Mutex<dyn GraphicsDevice>>> + Send + Sync + 'static,

Register a backend factory under a 'static name. Once registered, the application can pick that backend at runtime by passing the name to the plugin registry's create_graphics_device. Backends typically call this from their register() entry point.

Enregistre une factory de backend sous un nom 'static. Une fois enregistré, l'application peut choisir ce backend à l'exécution en passant le nom au create_graphics_device du plugin registry. Les backends appellent généralement ceci depuis leur point d'entrée register().

Example

Exemple

register_graphics_device_plugin("vulkan", |window, config| {
    let device = MyVulkanDevice::new(window, config)?;
    Ok(Arc::new(Mutex::new(device)))
});

DynamicRenderState

pub struct DynamicRenderState
Dynamic render state — resolved per draw call, no Vulkan dependency.
Carries all pipeline states that are set dynamically via vkCmdSet* instead of being baked into the pipeline at creation time. Built by the Material (one per pass) and passed to the backend as a single unit via CommandList::set_dynamic_state().
Dynamic render state — resolved per draw call, no Vulkan dependency.
Carries all pipeline states that are set dynamically via vkCmdSet* instead of being baked into the pipeline at creation time. Built by the Material (one per pass) and passed to the backend as a single unit via CommandList::set_dynamic_state().

struct

DynamicRenderStateKey

pub struct DynamicRenderStateKey
Hashable key for a DynamicRenderState.
DynamicRenderState contains f32 fields which do not implement Hash/Eq. This key stores the bit patterns of those floats (f32::to_bits()) so two equal states produce the same hash and compare equal. Used by ResourceManager::get_or_assign_material_render_state_signature_id() to deduplicate identical render states across materials.
Hashable key for a DynamicRenderState.
DynamicRenderState contains f32 fields which do not implement Hash/Eq. This key stores the bit patterns of those floats (f32::to_bits()) so two equal states produce the same hash and compare equal. Used by ResourceManager::get_or_assign_material_render_state_signature_id() to deduplicate identical render states across materials.

let x: DynamicRenderStateKey;

PipelineSignatureKey

pub struct PipelineSignatureKey
Identity of a pipeline layout: descriptor set layouts + push constant ranges.
Two pipelines with equal PipelineSignatureKey have compatible pipeline layouts per Vulkan spec §14.2.2, so their descriptor set binds can be shared when switching between them.
Identity of a pipeline layout: descriptor set layouts + push constant ranges.
Two pipelines with equal PipelineSignatureKey have compatible pipeline layouts per Vulkan spec §14.2.2, so their descriptor set binds can be shared when switching between them.

Example

Exemple

PipelineSignatureKey::from_reflection

pub fn from_reflection(reflection: &PipelineReflection) -> Self
Build a signature key from a pipeline reflection. Sets, bindings and push constant ranges are sorted for deterministic hashing.

Pipeline

pub trait Pipeline: Send + Sync
Pipeline resource trait
Implemented by backend-specific pipeline types. The pipeline is automatically destroyed when dropped. The pipeline stores its binding group layouts internally (Option B design).
Pipeline resource trait
Implemented by backend-specific pipeline types. The pipeline is automatically destroyed when dropped. The pipeline stores its binding group layouts internally (Option B design).

Example

Exemple

// Implement the trait or accept it as a bound:
fn use_it<T: Pipeline>(t: &T) { /* ... */ }

RenderPassDesc::color_resolve_attachments

pub color_resolve_attachments: Vec<AttachmentDesc>
Resolve attachments for MSAA color targets (empty if no MSAA). When present, the GPU automatically resolves each MSAA color attachment into the corresponding resolve attachment at the end of the render pass. Must have the same length as color_attachments or be empty.
Resolve attachments for MSAA color targets (empty if no MSAA). When present, the GPU automatically resolves each MSAA color attachment into the corresponding resolve attachment at the end of the render pass. Must have the same length as color_attachments or be empty.

Example

Exemple

let value = instance.color_resolve_attachments;

trait

Shader

pub trait Shader: Send + Sync
Shader resource trait
Implemented by backend-specific shader types (e.g., VulkanShader). The shader is automatically destroyed when dropped. Exposes SPIR-V reflection data so the backend can deduce descriptor set layouts and push constant ranges automatically.
Shader resource trait
Implemented by backend-specific shader types (e.g., VulkanShader). The shader is automatically destroyed when dropped. Exposes SPIR-V reflection data so the backend can deduce descriptor set layouts and push constant ranges automatically.

Example

Exemple

// Implement the trait or accept it as a bound:
fn use_it<T: Shader>(t: &T) { /* ... */ }

method

MipmapMode::max_mip_levels

pub fn max_mip_levels(width: u32, height: u32) -> u32
Calculate max possible mip levels for dimensions Returns floor(log2(max(width, height))) + 1
Calculate max possible mip levels for dimensions Returns floor(log2(max(width, height))) + 1

Example

Exemple

method

MipmapMode::mip_levels

pub fn mip_levels(&self, width: u32, height: u32) -> u32
Calculate actual mip levels for given dimensions
Calculate actual mip levels for given dimensions

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.mip_levels(/* args */);
variant

MipmapMode::Generate

Generate {
Generate mipmaps automatically on GPU using hardware blit
  • Generates full chain down to 1x1 by default
  • Optional max_levels limits the chain length
  • Quality: bilinear/box filter (fast but average quality)
Generate mipmaps automatically on GPU using hardware blit
  • Generates full chain down to 1x1 by default
  • Optional max_levels limits the chain length
  • Quality: bilinear/box filter (fast but average quality)

Example

Exemple

let v = MipmapMode::Generate;
variant

MipmapMode::Manual

Manual(ManualMipmapData)
Manually provided mipmap data (levels 1+) Level 0 comes from TextureData Use for: pre-processed assets with high-quality mipmaps (Lanczos, Kaiser)
Manually provided mipmap data (levels 1+) Level 0 comes from TextureData Use for: pre-processed assets with high-quality mipmaps (Lanczos, Kaiser)

Example

Exemple

let v = MipmapMode::Manual;
variant

MipmapMode::None

None
No mipmaps - only base level (mip_levels = 1) Use for: UI textures, render targets, procedural textures
No mipmaps - only base level (mip_levels = 1) Use for: UI textures, render targets, procedural textures

Example

Exemple

let v = MipmapMode::None;
enum

SamplerType

pub enum SamplerType
Predefined sampler configurations
Describes HOW a texture is sampled by the GPU (filtering, addressing, mipmapping). The backend creates and caches the actual GPU sampler objects internally.
Predefined sampler configurations
Describes HOW a texture is sampled by the GPU (filtering, addressing, mipmapping). The backend creates and caches the actual GPU sampler objects internally.

Example

Exemple

// Match on variants:
match value { SamplerType::Foo => /* ... */, _ => /* ... */ }
variant

SamplerType::Anisotropic

Anisotropic
Trilinear with max anisotropy, repeat addressing Best for: high-quality 3D textures at oblique angles
Trilinear with max anisotropy, repeat addressing Best for: high-quality 3D textures at oblique angles

Example

Exemple

let v = SamplerType::Anisotropic;
variant

SamplerType::LinearClamp

LinearClamp
Bilinear filtering, clamp-to-edge, trilinear mipmapping, anisotropy x16 Best for: UI, post-processing, environment maps
Bilinear filtering, clamp-to-edge, trilinear mipmapping, anisotropy x16 Best for: UI, post-processing, environment maps

Example

Exemple

let v = SamplerType::LinearClamp;
variant

SamplerType::LinearRepeat

LinearRepeat
Bilinear filtering, repeat addressing, trilinear mipmapping, anisotropy x16 Best for: most 3D textures (diffuse, normal, etc.)
Bilinear filtering, repeat addressing, trilinear mipmapping, anisotropy x16 Best for: most 3D textures (diffuse, normal, etc.)

Example

Exemple

let v = SamplerType::LinearRepeat;
variant

SamplerType::NearestClamp

NearestClamp
Nearest-neighbor filtering, clamp-to-edge, no anisotropy Best for: lookup tables, noise textures
Nearest-neighbor filtering, clamp-to-edge, no anisotropy Best for: lookup tables, noise textures

Example

Exemple

let v = SamplerType::NearestClamp;
variant

SamplerType::NearestRepeat

NearestRepeat
Nearest-neighbor filtering, repeat addressing, no anisotropy Best for: pixel art, voxel textures, data textures
Nearest-neighbor filtering, repeat addressing, no anisotropy Best for: pixel art, voxel textures, data textures

Example

Exemple

let v = SamplerType::NearestRepeat;
variant

SamplerType::Shadow

Shadow
Depth comparison sampler (linear, clamp-to-border white) Best for: shadow mapping
Depth comparison sampler (linear, clamp-to-border white) Best for: shadow mapping

Example

Exemple

let v = SamplerType::Shadow;

TextureData::Layers

Layers(Vec<TextureLayerData>)
Per-layer data for array textures. Only the layers listed are uploaded; others remain uninitialized. Allows full, partial, or empty upload.

galaxy_3d_engine::log

galaxy_3d_engine::log

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/log.rs
struct

DefaultLogger

pub struct DefaultLogger

The built-in console logger. Writes coloured output to stdout via the colored crate, prefixed by a millisecond timestamp. Activated automatically on the first Engine::log / Engine::log_detailed call. Replace it with Engine::set_logger(...).

Le logger console intégré. Écrit une sortie colorée sur stdout via le crate colored, préfixée d'un horodatage à la milliseconde. Activé automatiquement au premier appel de Engine::log / Engine::log_detailed. Remplaçable via Engine::set_logger(...).

Example

Exemple

// DefaultLogger is wired up automatically; no explicit setup needed.
// Just emit log messages through the macros:
galaxy_3d_engine::engine_info!("app", "booted in {} ms", elapsed.as_millis());
struct

LogEntry

pub struct LogEntry

One log message handed to Logger::log. Carries severity, timestamp, source string (e.g. "galaxy3d::Engine"), the formatted message, and optional file+line for Error-level entries.

Un message de log transmis à Logger::log. Contient la sévérité, l'horodatage, la chaîne source (par exemple "galaxy3d::Engine"), le message formaté, et optionnellement file+line pour les entrées de niveau Error.

Example

Exemple

// Inside a custom Logger:
fn log(&self, entry: &LogEntry) {
    if entry.severity >= LogSeverity::Warn {
        eprintln!("[{:?}] {}: {}", entry.severity, entry.source, entry.message);
    }
}

Source file (only for detailed ERROR logs)
Source file (only for detailed ERROR logs)

enum

LogSeverity

pub enum LogSeverity

Five-level severity ordering. From least to most severe: Trace, Debug, Info, Warn, Error. Implements Ord so loggers can filter by threshold via comparison.

Cinq niveaux de sévérité ordonnés. Du moins au plus sévère : Trace, Debug, Info, Warn, Error. Implémente Ord pour permettre aux loggers de filtrer par seuil via comparaison.

Example

Exemple

if entry.severity >= LogSeverity::Warn {
    // emit
}
variant

LogSeverity::Debug

Debug
Development/debugging information
Development/debugging information

Example

Exemple

let v = LogSeverity::Debug;
variant

LogSeverity::Error

Error
Error messages (critical issues with file:line details)
Error messages (critical issues with file:line details)

Example

Exemple

let v = LogSeverity::Error;
variant

LogSeverity::Info

Info
Important informational messages
Important informational messages

Example

Exemple

let v = LogSeverity::Info;
variant

LogSeverity::Trace

Trace
Very verbose debug information (typically disabled in release)
Very verbose debug information (typically disabled in release)

Example

Exemple

let v = LogSeverity::Trace;
variant

LogSeverity::Warn

Warn
Warning messages (potential issues)
Warning messages (potential issues)

Example

Exemple

let v = LogSeverity::Warn;
trait

Logger

pub trait Logger: Send + Sync

The single-method trait every logger backend must implement. Implementations must be Send + Sync because the global LOGGER is shared across threads.

Use Engine::set_logger to replace the default console logger.

Le trait à méthode unique que chaque backend de logger doit implémenter. Les implémentations doivent être Send + Sync car le LOGGER global est partagé entre threads.

Utilisez Engine::set_logger pour remplacer le logger console par défaut.

Example

Exemple

struct CapturingLogger(std::sync::Mutex<Vec<String>>);
impl Logger for CapturingLogger {
    fn log(&self, entry: &LogEntry) {
        self.0.lock().unwrap().push(entry.message.clone());
    }
}

galaxy_3d_engine::render_graph::access_type

galaxy_3d_engine::render_graph::access_type

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/render_graph/access_type.rs
struct

ResourceAccess

pub struct ResourceAccess

A pass's declaration of how it uses one resource: the graph_resource_key, the access_type (ColorAttachmentWrite, FragmentShaderRead, …), and an optional target_ops (Some only for attachment accesses on textures).

The render graph builds the DAG dependency edges from these (writer-before-reader); the backend builds vkCmdPipelineBarrier2 calls from the access types and previous-access tracking.

La déclaration faite par une passe sur l'usage qu'elle fait d'une ressource : le graph_resource_key, l'access_type (ColorAttachmentWrite, FragmentShaderRead…), et un target_ops optionnel (Some uniquement pour les accès attachement sur des textures).

Le render graph construit les arêtes du DAG depuis ces accès (writer avant reader) ; le backend construit les appels vkCmdPipelineBarrier2 depuis les access types et le suivi des accès précédents.

Example

Exemple

ResourceAccess {
    graph_resource_key: color_gr,
    access_type: AccessType::ColorAttachmentWrite,
    target_ops: Some(TargetOps::Color { clear_color: [0.0; 4], load_op: LoadOp::Clear, store_op: StoreOp::Store, resolve_target: None }),
}
field

ResourceAccess::access_type

pub access_type: AccessType
How the pass uses the resource.
How the pass uses the resource.

Example

Exemple

let value = instance.access_type;
field

ResourceAccess::graph_resource_key

pub graph_resource_key: super::graph_resource::GraphResourceKey
Key into RenderGraphManager::graph_resources.
Key into RenderGraphManager::graph_resources.

Example

Exemple

let value = instance.graph_resource_key;
field

ResourceAccess::target_ops

pub target_ops: Option<TargetOps>
Attachment ops — Some only for Color* / DepthStencil* access types targeting a texture, None otherwise.
Attachment ops — Some only for Color* / DepthStencil* access types targeting a texture, None otherwise.

Example

Exemple

let value = instance.target_ops;
enum

TargetOps

pub enum TargetOps

Per-pass × per-attachment configuration. Two variants: Color { clear_color, load_op, store_op, resolve_target } for color attachments (resolve_target is Some for MSAA resolve; either all color attachments of a pass have a resolve, or none — Vulkan rejects mixed cases) and DepthStencil { ... } with separate load/store ops for depth and stencil.

Lives on ResourceAccess, not on the resource itself, because the same texture is typically cleared by the first pass and loaded by every subsequent pass.

Configuration par passe × par attachement. Deux variantes : Color { clear_color, load_op, store_op, resolve_target } pour les attachements couleur (resolve_target vaut Some pour le resolve MSAA ; toutes les attachements couleur d'une passe ont un resolve, ou aucune — Vulkan rejette les cas mixtes) et DepthStencil { ... } avec des ops load/store séparées pour depth et stencil.

Vit sur ResourceAccess, pas sur la ressource elle-même, parce que la même texture est typiquement clear par la première passe et load par toutes les suivantes.

Example

Exemple

let ops = TargetOps::Color {
    clear_color: [0.05, 0.05, 0.08, 1.0],
    load_op: LoadOp::Clear, store_op: StoreOp::Store,
    resolve_target: None,
};
variant

TargetOps::Color

Color {
Configuration for a color attachment.
Configuration for a color attachment.

Example

Exemple

let v = TargetOps::Color;
variant

TargetOps::DepthStencil

DepthStencil {
Configuration for a depth/stencil attachment.
Configuration for a depth/stencil attachment.

Example

Exemple

let v = TargetOps::DepthStencil;

ColorAttachmentSlot

pub struct ColorAttachmentSlot
One color attachment slot inside a framebuffer: a color GraphResource plus its optional MSAA resolve target.
Pairing color and resolve in the same struct preserves their association by construction (vs two parallel Vecs) and makes the lookup key trivially hashable.

galaxy_3d_engine::render_graph::graph_resource

galaxy_3d_engine::render_graph::graph_resource

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/render_graph/graph_resource.rs
struct

GraphResourceKey

pub struct GraphResourceKey
Stable key for a GraphResource in the RenderGraphManager.
Stable key for a GraphResource in the RenderGraphManager.

Example

Exemple

// Use the type in a function signature or storage:
let x: GraphResourceKey;
enum

GraphResource

pub enum GraphResource

A typed reference to a CPU resource the render graph operates on. Two variants:

  • Texture { texture_key, base_mip_level, base_array_layer, layer_count } — the texture key plus the sub-resource view to bind.
  • Buffer(BufferKey) — a buffer reference (no sub-resource).

Holds keys, not Arcs, so the graph stays independent of resource lifetimes — passes can swap their target at runtime by changing only the key.

Une référence typée à une ressource CPU sur laquelle le render graph opère. Deux variantes :

  • Texture { texture_key, base_mip_level, base_array_layer, layer_count } — la clé de texture plus la vue de sous-ressource à binder.
  • Buffer(BufferKey) — une référence à un buffer (pas de sous-ressource).

Contient des clés, pas des Arc, pour que le graph reste indépendant du cycle de vie des ressources — les passes peuvent changer leur cible à l'exécution en ne changeant que la clé.

Example

Exemple

let color_gr = rgm.create_graph_resource("color", GraphResource::Texture {
    texture_key,
    base_mip_level: 0,
    base_array_layer: 0,
    layer_count: 1,
})?;
method

GraphResource::is_buffer

pub fn is_buffer(self) -> bool
True if this resource is a buffer.
True if this resource is a buffer.

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.is_buffer(/* args */);

True if this resource is a texture.
True if this resource is a texture.

References a Texture in the ResourceManager. The fields below describe the subresource range targeted when this resource is used as a framebuffer attachment (mirrors FramebufferAttachment).

galaxy_3d_engine::render_graph::pass_action

galaxy_3d_engine::render_graph::pass_action

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/render_graph/pass_action.rs
struct

CustomAction

pub struct CustomAction

Closure-based PassAction for custom recording logic. The boxed closure receives (cmd, pass_info) and can issue any sequence of binds / draws / push constants.

PassAction basée sur une closure pour une logique d'enregistrement personnalisée. La closure boxée reçoit (cmd, pass_info) et peut émettre n'importe quelle séquence de binds / draws / push constants.

Example

Exemple

let action = CustomAction::new(|cmd, _info| {
    cmd.bind_pipeline(&pipeline)?;
    cmd.draw(particle_count, 0)?;
    Ok(())
});
method

CustomAction::new

pub fn new<F>(callback: F) -> Self where F: FnMut(&mut dyn CommandList, &PassInfo) -> Result<()> + Send + Sync + 'static,
Create a new CustomAction.
Crée un nouveau CustomAction.

Example

Exemple

let result = CustomAction::new(/* args */)?;
struct

FullscreenAction

pub struct FullscreenAction

Data-driven fullscreen-triangle pass: binds a pipeline, binds a binding group at set 0, draws three vertices. Ideal for tonemapping, post-processing, deferred shading composition, etc.

Passe de triangle plein-écran data-driven : bind un pipeline, bind un binding group au set 0, draw trois sommets. Idéal pour le tonemapping, le post-processing, la composition de deferred shading, etc.

Example

Exemple

let action = FullscreenAction::new(tonemap_pipeline, tonemap_bg);
method

FullscreenAction::new

pub fn new( pipeline: Arc<dyn graphics_device::Pipeline>, binding_group: Arc<dyn graphics_device::BindingGroup>, ) -> Self
Create a new FullscreenAction.
Crée un nouveau FullscreenAction.

Example

Exemple

let result = FullscreenAction::new(/* args */);
struct

ScenePassAction

pub struct ScenePassAction

Scene-rendering PassAction. Owns Arc<Mutex<Scene>> + Arc<Mutex<dyn Drawer>> + Arc<Mutex<Option<RenderView>>>; on execute it locks all three and forwards to drawer.draw(scene, view, cmd, pass_info, &binding_group, bind_textures). The set-1 binding group is built eagerly at construction.

PassAction de rendu de scène. Possède Arc<Mutex<Scene>> + Arc<Mutex<dyn Drawer>> + Arc<Mutex<Option<RenderView>>> ; à l'execute, verrouille les trois et délègue à drawer.draw(scene, view, cmd, pass_info, &binding_group, bind_textures). Le binding group du set 1 est construit en eager à la construction.

Example

Exemple

let action = ScenePassAction::new(
    scene.clone(), drawer.clone(), render_view.clone(),
    vec![
        SceneBinding::UniformBuffer(frame_buf),
        SceneBinding::StorageBuffer(instance_buf),
        SceneBinding::StorageBuffer(material_buf),
        SceneBinding::StorageBuffer(light_buf),
    ],
    /* bind_textures */ true,
)?;
method

ScenePassAction::new

pub fn new( scene: Arc<Mutex<Scene>>, drawer: Arc<Mutex<dyn Drawer>>, render_view: Arc<Mutex<Option<RenderView>>>, bindings: Vec<SceneBinding>, bind_textures: bool, ) -> Result<Self>
Create a ScenePassAction.
bindings is the list of buffers/textures for the per-pass descriptor set (set 1). The BindingGroup is created immediately — no lazy, no pipeline needed.
Create a ScenePassAction.
bindings is the list of buffers/textures for the per-pass descriptor set (set 1). The BindingGroup is created immediately — no lazy, no pipeline needed.

Example

Exemple

let result = ScenePassAction::new(/* args */)?;

SceneBinding

pub enum SceneBinding
A binding resource for the scene pass descriptor set.
Each entry maps to a binding index (0, 1, 2, ...) in declaration order.

// Match on variants:
match value { SceneBinding::Foo => /* ... */, _ => /* ... */ }

SceneBinding::UniformBuffer

UniformBuffer(Arc<Buffer>)
Uniform buffer
Uniform buffer

trait

PassAction

pub trait PassAction: Send + Sync

Trait for the pass-recording strategy: execute(cmd, pass_info) records the pass's draw commands between the engine-managed begin_render_pass / end_render_pass. Three implementations are provided — FullscreenAction (3-vertex triangle), CustomAction (closure-based), ScenePassAction (delegates to a Drawer).

Trait pour la stratégie d'enregistrement d'une passe : execute(cmd, pass_info) enregistre les commandes de draw de la passe entre les begin_render_pass / end_render_pass gérés par le moteur. Trois implémentations sont fournies — FullscreenAction (triangle à 3 sommets), CustomAction (basée sur une closure), ScenePassAction (délègue à un Drawer).

Example

Exemple

let action: Box<dyn PassAction> = Box::new(CustomAction::new(|cmd, _info| {
    cmd.bind_pipeline(&fs_pipeline)?;
    cmd.bind_binding_group(&fs_pipeline, 0, &fs_bg)?;
    cmd.draw(3, 0)
}));

galaxy_3d_engine::render_graph::render_graph

galaxy_3d_engine::render_graph::render_graph

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/render_graph/render_graph.rs
struct

RenderGraph

pub struct RenderGraph

One render graph: a name, a ring of frames_in_flight command lists, and persistent scratch buffers (sorted-pass list, previous-access map, image/buffer access lists, topo-sort scratch). Created and owned by the RenderGraphManager.

The execute pipeline runs in five phases: (1) lock the RM, (2) validate keys, (3) topological sort, (4) advance ring + cmd.begin(), (5) for each sorted pass record barriers + begin_render_pass + action + end_render_pass, then post-pass closure, then cmd.end().

Un render graph : un nom, un anneau de frames_in_flight command lists et des scratch buffers persistants (liste des passes triées, map des accès précédents, listes d'accès image/buffer, scratch du tri topologique). Créé et possédé par le RenderGraphManager.

Le pipeline d'exécution se déroule en cinq phases : (1) verrouiller le RM, (2) valider les clés, (3) tri topologique, (4) avancer l'anneau + cmd.begin(), (5) pour chaque passe triée enregistrer les barrières + begin_render_pass + action + end_render_pass, puis la closure post-pass, puis cmd.end().

Example

Exemple

// Created by the manager:
let graph_key = rgm.create_render_graph("main", 2)?;
let rg: &RenderGraph = rgm.render_graph(graph_key).unwrap();
let cmd: &dyn CommandList = rg.command_list()?;
method

RenderGraph::command_list

pub fn command_list(&self) -> Result<&dyn graphics_device::CommandList>
Borrow the command list recorded by the most recent execute() call.
Borrow the command list recorded by the most recent execute() call.

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.command_list(/* args */)?;

Method of the RenderGraph type.
Méthode du type RenderGraph.

Example

Exemple

Stable key for a RenderGraph in the RenderGraphManager.

galaxy_3d_engine::render_graph::render_graph_manager

galaxy_3d_engine::render_graph::render_graph_manager

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/render_graph/render_graph_manager.rs
struct

RenderGraphManager

pub struct RenderGraphManager

The central authority for the engine's frame DAG. Owns four collections by stable keys: render graphs, render passes, graph resources, framebuffers (the latter is unnamed and content-addressed via FramebufferLookupKey).

Eager-validation contract: every mutation to a pass's accesses (set_pass_access_resource, set_pass_access_target_ops, replace_pass_accesses) immediately rebuilds the pass's cache (framebuffer, render-pass descriptor, pass info, clear values). Validation errors surface at the call site, never at frame execute time.

Drive frames via execute_render_graph(graph_key, &[passes], post_passes).

L'autorité centrale du DAG par frame du moteur. Possède quatre collections indexées par clés stables : render graphs, render passes, graph resources, framebuffers (les framebuffers étant sans nom et content-addressés via FramebufferLookupKey).

Contrat de validation eager : chaque mutation des accès d'une passe (set_pass_access_resource, set_pass_access_target_ops, replace_pass_accesses) reconstruit immédiatement le cache de la passe (framebuffer, descripteur de render pass, pass info, clear values). Les erreurs de validation remontent au call site, jamais au moment de l'exécution.

Lancez les frames via execute_render_graph(graph_key, &[passes], post_passes).

Example

Exemple

let rgm_arc = Engine::render_graph_manager()?;
let mut rgm = rgm_arc.lock().unwrap();
let graph = rgm.create_render_graph("main", /* frames in flight */ 2)?;
let color = rgm.create_graph_resource("color", GraphResource::Texture {
    texture_key, base_mip_level: 0, base_array_layer: 0, layer_count: 1,
})?;
let opaque = rgm.create_render_pass("opaque", vec![/* accesses */], action)?;
method

RenderGraphManager::clear

pub fn clear(&mut self)
Drop every element held by this RenderGraphManager.
Vide ce RenderGraphManager de tous ses éléments.

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.clear(/* args */);
method

RenderGraphManager::create_graph_resource

pub fn create_graph_resource( &mut self, name: &str, resource: GraphResource, ) -> Result<GraphResourceKey>
Method of the RenderGraphManager type.
Méthode du type RenderGraphManager.

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.create_graph_resource(/* args */)?;
method

RenderGraphManager::create_render_graph

pub fn create_render_graph( &mut self, name: &str, frames_in_flight: usize, ) -> Result<RenderGraphKey>

Create a named render graph backed by a ring of frames_in_flight command lists. Returns a stable RenderGraphKey. Bails if the name is already taken.

Crée un render graph nommé adossé à un anneau de frames_in_flight command lists. Retourne un RenderGraphKey stable. Échoue si le nom est déjà utilisé.

Example

Exemple

let graph_key = rgm.create_render_graph("main", 2)?;
method

RenderGraphManager::create_render_pass

pub fn create_render_pass( &mut self, name: &str, accesses: Vec<ResourceAccess>, action: Box<dyn PassAction>, ) -> Result<RenderPassKey>

Create a render pass with a list of ResourceAccess records and a PassAction. Eagerly builds the pass cache (framebuffer, render pass descriptor, pass info, clear values); validation errors (mismatched sample counts, mixed MSAA resolves, buffer used as color attachment) surface here.

Crée une render pass avec une liste de ResourceAccess et une PassAction. Construit immédiatement le cache de la passe (framebuffer, descripteur de render pass, pass info, clear values) ; les erreurs de validation (sample counts incompatibles, resolves MSAA mixés, buffer utilisé comme attachement couleur) remontent ici.

Example

Exemple

let pass_key = rgm.create_render_pass("opaque", vec![
    ResourceAccess {
        graph_resource_key: color_gr,
        access_type: AccessType::ColorAttachmentWrite,
        target_ops: Some(TargetOps::Color {
            clear_color: [0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0],
            load_op: LoadOp::Clear, store_op: StoreOp::Store,
            resolve_target: None,
        }),
    },
], Box::new(scene_action))?;
method

RenderGraphManager::execute_render_graph

pub fn execute_render_graph<F>( &mut self, graph_key: RenderGraphKey, passes: &[RenderPassKey], post_passes: F, ) -> Result<()> where F: FnOnce(&mut dyn graphics_device::CommandList) -> Result<()>,

Execute one frame of a render graph. Provide the list of passes to run (subset of those registered) and a post_passes closure that runs after the last pass within the same command list (typical use: blitting the offscreen color into the swapchain image).

The render graph topologically sorts the passes (writer-before-reader), records each one's barriers + dynamic-rendering begin / action / end, then your post-pass closure, all into the next command list of the ring. The ResourceManager lock is held only briefly during access materialization and released before any user callback.

Exécute une frame d'un render graph. Fournissez la liste des passes à lancer (sous-ensemble de celles enregistrées) et une closure post_passes qui s'exécute après la dernière passe dans la même command list (usage typique : blit du color offscreen vers l'image du swapchain).

Le render graph trie topologiquement les passes (writer avant reader), enregistre les barrières + dynamic-rendering begin / action / end de chacune, puis votre closure post-pass, le tout dans la prochaine command list de l'anneau. Le verrou du ResourceManager n'est tenu que brièvement pendant la matérialisation des accès et libéré avant toute callback utilisateur.

Example

Exemple

rgm.execute_render_graph(graph_key, &[opaque_key, transparent_key], |cmd| {
    swapchain.record_present_blit(cmd, offscreen_color, image_index)?;
    Ok(())
})?;
method

RenderGraphManager::get_or_create_framebuffer

pub fn get_or_create_framebuffer( &mut self, color_attachments: &[ColorAttachmentSlot], depth_stencil_attachment: Option<GraphResourceKey>, ) -> Result<FramebufferKey>
Get or create a Framebuffer matching the given attachment set.
Idempotent: identical inputs return the same FramebufferKey across calls. Validates dimensions, layer counts, sample counts, resolve consistency (all-or-none), and that every GraphResourceKey is a Texture (not a Buffer).
Get or create a Framebuffer matching the given attachment set.
Idempotent: identical inputs return the same FramebufferKey across calls. Validates dimensions, layer counts, sample counts, resolve consistency (all-or-none), and that every GraphResourceKey is a Texture (not a Buffer).

Example

RenderGraphManager::replace_pass_accesses

pub fn replace_pass_accesses( &mut self, pass_key: RenderPassKey, new_accesses: Vec<ResourceAccess>, ) -> Result<()>
Replace every ResourceAccess of the pass at once. Useful when the access set changes structurally (e.g. toggling MSAA) — using the per-access setters one by one would walk through invalid intermediate states (mixed resolves, etc.) and create transient framebuffers we don't actually want.
Replace every ResourceAccess of the pass at once. Useful when the access set changes structurally (e.g. toggling MSAA) — using the per-access setters one by one would walk through invalid intermediate states (mixed resolves, etc.) and create transient framebuffers we don't actually want.

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.replace_pass_accesses(/* args */)?;
method

galaxy_3d_engine::render_graph::render_pass

galaxy_3d_engine::render_graph::render_pass

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/render_graph/render_pass.rs
struct

RenderPass

pub struct RenderPass

One DAG node — a single render pass. Holds the pass name, a Vec<ResourceAccess> describing every resource the pass touches, a boxed PassAction, and four eagerly-cached fields (framebuffer_key, pass_info, gd_render_pass, clear_values) rebuilt by the manager on every access mutation.

Un nœud du DAG — une seule render pass. Contient le nom de la passe, un Vec<ResourceAccess> décrivant chaque ressource que la passe touche, une PassAction boxée, et quatre champs cachés en eager (framebuffer_key, pass_info, gd_render_pass, clear_values) reconstruits par le manager à chaque mutation d'accès.

Example

Exemple

let pass = rgm.render_pass(pass_key).unwrap();
assert!(pass.framebuffer_key().is_some());
method

galaxy_3d_engine::resource::buffer

galaxy_3d_engine::resource::buffer

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/resource/buffer.rs
struct

Buffer

pub struct Buffer

Resource-level wrapper around a graphics_device::Buffer with std140 / std430 field metadata. Build via ResourceManager::create_buffer with a BufferKind (Uniform or Storage) and an array of FieldDesc; the wrapper computes per-field offsets automatically following the appropriate alignment rules.

Update via update_field(element_index, field_index, &bytes) for type-safe targeted writes, or update_element(element_index, &bytes) for whole-element writes.

Wrapper niveau ressource autour d'un graphics_device::Buffer avec métadonnées de champs std140 / std430. Construit via ResourceManager::create_buffer avec un BufferKind (Uniform ou Storage) et un tableau de FieldDesc ; le wrapper calcule automatiquement les offsets de chaque champ en suivant les règles d'alignement appropriées.

Mise à jour via update_field(index_élément, index_champ, &octets) pour des écritures ciblées typées, ou update_element(index_élément, &octets) pour des écritures d'élément complet.

Example

Exemple

let key = rm.create_buffer("counters".to_string(), BufferDesc {
    graphics_device: gd_arc.clone(),
    kind: BufferKind::Storage,
    fields: vec![
        FieldDesc { name: "hits".into(),  field_type: FieldType::UInt },
        FieldDesc { name: "miss".into(),  field_type: FieldType::UInt },
    ],
    count: 1,
})?;
let buf = rm.buffer(key).unwrap();
buf.update_field(0, /* hits index */ 0, bytemuck::bytes_of(&42u32))?;
method

Buffer::buffer_ptr

pub unsafe fn buffer_ptr(&self) -> Option<*mut u8>
Raw pointer to the beginning of the mapped buffer

Safety


  • No synchronization with GPU reads — caller must ensure
the GPU is not reading this buffer (e.g. double buffering)
  • No bounds checking on subsequent writes
Raw pointer to the beginning of the mapped buffer

Safety


  • No synchronization with GPU reads — caller must ensure
the GPU is not reading this buffer (e.g. double buffering)
  • No bounds checking on subsequent writes

Example

Exemple

Raw pointer to the start of element at index

Safety


  • No synchronization with GPU reads
  • No bounds checking on subsequent writes
  • Caller must ensure index < count

Safety

  • No bounds checking on subsequent writes
  • Caller must ensure index < count

galaxy_3d_engine::resource::geometry

galaxy_3d_engine::resource::geometry

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/resource/geometry.rs
struct

Geometry

pub struct Geometry

The shared GPU geometry of a model. Owns one big vertex buffer + one index buffer (Arc-shared); the meshes vector contains GeometryMeshes, each with a list of GeometrySubMeshes, each with its LOD chain (GeometrySubMeshLODs holding offsets into the shared buffers + a topology + lod_thresholds for hysteresis).

La géométrie GPU partagée d'un modèle. Possède un gros vertex buffer + un index buffer (partagés via Arc) ; le vecteur meshes contient des GeometryMesh, chacun avec une liste de GeometrySubMesh, chacun avec sa chaîne de LODs (GeometrySubMeshLOD contenant les offsets dans les buffers partagés + une topology + lod_thresholds pour l'hystérésis).

Example

Exemple

let geo_key = rm.create_geometry("player_geo".to_string(), GeometryDesc {
    name: "player_geo".into(),
    graphics_device: gd_arc.clone(),
    vertex_data, index_data: Some(index_data),
    vertex_layout, index_type: IndexType::U16,
    meshes: vec![/* GeometryMeshDesc { ... } */],
})?;
method

Geometry::add_mesh

pub fn add_mesh(&mut self, desc: GeometryMeshDesc) -> Result<usize>
Add a mesh, returns its id (index).
Validates all submesh LOD offsets against buffer sizes.
Add a mesh, returns its id (index).
Validates all submesh LOD offsets against buffer sizes.

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.add_mesh(/* args */)?;
method

Geometry::add_submesh

pub fn add_submesh( &mut self, mesh_id: usize, desc: GeometrySubMeshDesc, ) -> Result<usize>
Add a submesh (with all its LODs) to an existing mesh, returns the submesh id.
Add a submesh (with all its LODs) to an existing mesh, returns the submesh id.

Example
