let result = instance.add_submesh(/* args */)?;
method

Geometry::add_submesh_lod

pub fn add_submesh_lod( &mut self, mesh_id: usize, submesh_id: usize, desc: GeometrySubMeshLODDesc, threshold: Option<(f32, f32)>, ) -> Result<usize>
Add a single LOD variant to an existing submesh, returns the new lod index.
threshold is the (drop, raise) pair for the new frontier introduced between the previously last LOD and the one being added. It must be None when adding the very first LOD (no frontier), and Some for any subsequent LOD.
Add a single LOD variant to an existing submesh, returns the new lod index.
threshold is the (drop, raise) pair for the new frontier introduced between the previously last LOD and the one being added. It must be None when adding the very first LOD (no frontier), and Some for any subsequent LOD.

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.add_submesh_lod(/* args */)?;
method

Get the graphics device reference

GeometryDesc

pub struct GeometryDesc
Descriptor for creating a Geometry resource
The ResourceManager will create the GPU buffers from the provided data. Vertex and index counts are computed automatically from data length and layout.

The ResourceManager will create the GPU buffers from the provided data. Vertex and index counts are computed automatically from data length and layout.

GeometryMesh

pub struct GeometryMesh
A named mesh within a Geometry group.
Contains multiple submeshes (named drawable components), each with its own LOD chain. Submeshes are stable across LODs — the LOD selection happens per-submesh, not per-mesh.
A named mesh within a Geometry group.
Contains multiple submeshes (named drawable components), each with its own LOD chain. Submeshes are stable across LODs — the LOD selection happens per-submesh, not per-mesh.

GeometrySubMesh

pub struct GeometrySubMesh
A named drawable component of a GeometryMesh (e.g., "body", "armor").
Contains one or more LOD variants. The submesh identity is stable across LODs — only the geometric detail varies. A submesh may have fewer LODs than other submeshes in the same GeometryMesh (e.g., a cape that disappears at the lowest LOD).
A named drawable component of a GeometryMesh (e.g., "body", "armor").
Contains one or more LOD variants. The submesh identity is stable across LODs — only the geometric detail varies. A submesh may have fewer LODs than other submeshes in the same GeometryMesh (e.g., a cape that disappears at the lowest LOD).

Exemple

GeometrySubMeshDesc

pub struct GeometrySubMeshDesc
Descriptor for creating a GeometrySubMesh (with all its LOD variants).
Descriptor for creating a GeometrySubMesh (with all its LOD variants).

Example

Exemple

// Use the type in a function signature or storage:
let x: GeometrySubMeshDesc;
field

GeometrySubMeshDesc::lod_thresholds

pub lod_thresholds: Vec<(f32, f32)>
LOD transition thresholds, one pair per frontier between consecutive LODs. Size must be lods.len() - 1. Each pair is (drop, raise) in pixels of projected sphere diameter: drop is the screen size under which the renderer switches to the coarser LOD; raise > drop is the screen size above which it comes back to the finer one (hysteresis).
Thresholds must be strictly decreasing across frontiers (thresholds[i].0 > thresholds[i+1].1) so each LOD maps to a contiguous screen-size interval.
LOD transition thresholds, one pair per frontier between consecutive LODs. Size must be lods.len() - 1. Each pair is (drop, raise) in pixels of projected sphere diameter: drop is the screen size under which the renderer switches to the coarser LOD; raise > drop is the screen size above which it comes back to the finer one (hysteresis).
Thresholds must be strictly decreasing across frontiers (thresholds[i].0 > thresholds[i+1].1) so each LOD maps to a contiguous screen-size interval.

Example

Exemple

let value = instance.lod_thresholds;
field

pub lods: Vec<GeometrySubMeshLODDesc>
LOD variants (index 0 = most detailed). Submeshes may have different LOD counts (e.g., a cape may have fewer LODs than the body).
LOD variants (index 0 = most detailed). Submeshes may have different LOD counts (e.g., a cape may have fewer LODs than the body).

galaxy_3d_engine::resource::material

galaxy_3d_engine::resource::material

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/resource/material.rs
struct

Material

pub struct Material

A multi-pass material. Holds a global slot_id: u32 for the material SSBO, a Vec<MaterialPass> indexed by pass-type (each pass has its own fragment shader, blend state, polygon mode, dynamic state, textures and parameters), and a generation counter that bumps on parameter changes affecting GPU layout — used by RenderSubMeshPass::is_pipeline_valid to invalidate cached pipelines.

Un matériau multi-passes. Détient un slot_id: u32 global pour le SSBO de matériaux, un Vec<MaterialPass> indexé par pass-type (chaque passe a son fragment shader, blend state, polygon mode, état dynamique, textures et paramètres propres), et un compteur generation incrémenté lors des changements de paramètres affectant la disposition GPU — utilisé par RenderSubMeshPass::is_pipeline_valid pour invalider les pipelines cachés.

Example

Exemple

let mat_key = rm.create_material("steel".to_string(), MaterialDesc {
    passes: vec![MaterialPassDesc {
        pass_type: PASS_OPAQUE,
        fragment_shader: fs_key,
        color_blend: ColorBlendState::default(),
        polygon_mode: PolygonMode::Fill,
        textures: vec![],
        params: vec![
            ("baseColor".into(), ParamValue::Vec3([0.7, 0.7, 0.7])),
            ("metallic".into(), ParamValue::Float(0.9)),
        ],
        render_state: None,
    }],
}, &*gd)?;
method

Material::generation

pub fn generation(&self) -> u64
Get the generation counter (for pipeline cache invalidation)
Get the generation counter (for pipeline cache invalidation)

method

Material::iter_all_params

pub fn iter_all_params(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &MaterialParam>
Iterate over ALL parameters across all passes (for SSBO upload).
Order is: pass 0 params, then pass 1 params, etc. Since param names are globally unique across passes, this iterator yields the union without duplicates.
Iterate over ALL parameters across all passes (for SSBO upload).
Order is: pass 0 params, then pass 1 params, etc. Since param names are globally unique across passes, this iterator yields the union without duplicates.

Iterate over ALL texture slots across all passes (for SSBO upload).
Order is: pass 0 slots, then pass 1 slots, etc. Since slot names are globally unique across passes, this iterator yields the union without duplicates.
Iterate over ALL texture slots across all passes (for SSBO upload).
Order is: pass 0 slots, then pass 1 slots, etc. Since slot names are globally unique across passes, this iterator yields the union without duplicates.

MaterialPass

pub struct MaterialPass
A single rendering pass within a Material.
Each pass has its own pipeline state and its own subset of texture slots and parameters. Texture slot names and parameter names are globally unique across all passes of a Material — a given name appears in exactly one pass.
A single rendering pass within a Material.
Each pass has its own pipeline state and its own subset of texture slots and parameters. Texture slot names and parameter names are globally unique across all passes of a Material — a given name appears in exactly one pass.

MaterialPass::render_state_signature_id

pub fn render_state_signature_id(&self) -> u16
Get the stable u16 id identifying this pass's render state.
Two passes with identical DynamicRenderState values share the same id. Used by the drawer to skip redundant set_dynamic_state emissions.
Get the stable u16 id identifying this pass's render state.
Two passes with identical DynamicRenderState values share the same id. Used by the drawer to skip redundant set_dynamic_state emissions.

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.render_state_signature_id(/* args */);

MaterialTextureSlot

pub struct MaterialTextureSlot
A texture bound to a named slot in a material pass (resolved indices)
After creation, layer and region references are resolved to u32 indices. The texture is stored as a key, resolved via the ResourceManager.

After creation, layer and region references are resolved to u32 indices. The texture is stored as a key, resolved via the ResourceManager.

galaxy_3d_engine::resource::mesh

galaxy_3d_engine::resource::mesh

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/resource/mesh.rs
struct

Mesh

pub struct Mesh

A renderable composition: references a Geometry, picks one of its GeometryMeshes by index or name, and assigns a Material to each submesh. The mesh resolves all references at construction; the resulting indices give O(1) lookup at draw time.

Une composition renderable : référence une Geometry, sélectionne l'un de ses GeometryMesh par index ou nom, et assigne un Material à chaque submesh. Le mesh résout toutes les références à la construction ; les indices résultants permettent un lookup O(1) au moment du draw.

Example

Exemple

let mesh_key = rm.create_mesh("player".to_string(), MeshDesc {
    geometry: geo_key,
    geometry_mesh: GeometryMeshRef::Name("body".to_string()),
    submeshes: vec![
        MeshSubMeshDesc { submesh: GeometrySubMeshRef::Name("main".into()), material: steel_key },
    ],
})?;
method

Get the geometry key

MeshSubMesh

pub struct MeshSubMesh
A submesh with its assigned material (resolved).
Maps a GeometrySubMesh (by id) to a Material. All LOD variants of the referenced GeometrySubMesh share this Material.
Entries in Mesh::submeshes are ordered to match the parent GeometryMesh submesh order.
A submesh with its assigned material (resolved).
Maps a GeometrySubMesh (by id) to a Material. All LOD variants of the referenced GeometrySubMesh share this Material.
Entries in Mesh::submeshes are ordered to match the parent GeometryMesh submesh order.

fn

mesh_desc_from_name_mapping

pub fn mesh_desc_from_name_mapping( geometry: GeometryKey, geometry_mesh: GeometryMeshRef, name_to_material: &FxHashMap<String, MaterialKey>, resource_manager: &ResourceManager, ) -> Result<MeshDesc>
Build a MeshDesc by mapping submesh names to materials.
Iterates the submeshes of the referenced GeometryMesh and assigns materials based on names.

Errors


Returns an error if:
  • The geometry key is not found in the ResourceManager
  • The geometry_mesh reference is invalid
  • A submesh name is not found in the mapping
Build a MeshDesc by mapping submesh names to materials.
Iterates the submeshes of the referenced GeometryMesh and assigns materials based on names.

Errors


Returns an error if:
  • The geometry key is not found in the ResourceManager
  • The geometry_mesh reference is invalid
  • A submesh name is not found in the mapping

Example

Exemple

let result = mesh_desc_from_name_mapping(/* args */)?;

galaxy_3d_engine::resource::pipeline

galaxy_3d_engine::resource::pipeline

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/resource/pipeline.rs
struct

Pipeline

pub struct Pipeline

Resource-level pipeline. Wraps an Arc<dyn graphics_device::Pipeline> plus two 16-bit ids — signature_id (shared across pipelines that have compatible descriptor-set layouts; the drawer skips redundant set 0/1 rebinds when this stays the same) and sort_id (unique per pipeline).

Pipeline au niveau ressource. Encapsule un Arc<dyn graphics_device::Pipeline> plus deux ids 16 bits — signature_id (partagé entre pipelines aux layouts de descriptor-sets compatibles ; le drawer évite les rebinds redondants des sets 0/1 quand il reste constant) et sort_id (unique par pipeline).

Example

Exemple

// Pipelines are usually fetched via the cache:
let pk = rm.resolve_pipeline(/* ... */)?;
let pipeline = rm.pipeline(pk).unwrap();
method

struct

PipelineDesc

pub struct PipelineDesc
Pipeline creation descriptor
Shaders are referenced by ShaderKey; the ResourceManager resolves them and passes the GPU shader objects to the backend. Push constant ranges and descriptor set layouts are deduced automatically from shader reflection.
Pipeline creation descriptor
Shaders are referenced by ShaderKey; the ResourceManager resolves them and passes the GPU shader objects to the backend. Push constant ranges and descriptor set layouts are deduced automatically from shader reflection.

struct

PassInfo

pub struct PassInfo
Render pass attachment information needed for pipeline creation.
Derived automatically by RenderGraph::compile() from the resolved render pass attachments. Carries a generation counter that is incremented only when the actual formats or sample count change.
Also accepted as a manual construction (e.g. in tests or demos) via PassInfo::new().
Render pass attachment information needed for pipeline creation.
Derived automatically by RenderGraph::compile() from the resolved render pass attachments. Carries a generation counter that is incremented only when the actual formats or sample count change.
Also accepted as a manual construction (e.g. in tests or demos) via PassInfo::new().

struct

PipelineCacheKey

pub struct PipelineCacheKey
Composite key for the pipeline cache.
Contains all parameters that uniquely identify a Vulkan pipeline. Used as HashMap key — Rust's HashMap computes the hash internally and uses Eq for collision resolution.
Composite key for the pipeline cache.
Contains all parameters that uniquely identify a Vulkan pipeline. Used as HashMap key — Rust's HashMap computes the hash internally and uses Eq for collision resolution.

PipelineCacheKey::vertex_layout

pub vertex_layout: Arc<graphics_device::VertexLayout>
Arc-wrapped vertex layout: Hash and Eq delegate to the inner content, so two Arcs pointing to identical layouts compare equal and hash the same. Cloning the Arc is a single atomic increment (zero allocation).
Arc-wrapped vertex layout: Hash and Eq delegate to the inner content, so two Arcs pointing to identical layouts compare equal and hash the same. Cloning the Arc is a single atomic increment (zero allocation).

let value = instance.vertex_shader;
struct

PipelineKey

pub struct PipelineKey
Stable key for a Pipeline in the ResourceManager.
Stable key for a Pipeline in the ResourceManager.

Example

Exemple

// Use the type in a function signature or storage:
let x: PipelineKey;
struct

ResourceManager

pub struct ResourceManager

The central CPU-side resource registry. Owns seven kinds of resources keyed by stable slot-map keys: TextureKey, GeometryKey, ShaderKey, PipelineKey, MaterialKey, MeshKey, BufferKey. Each kind exposes the same accessor pattern: thing(key) (fast path), thing_by_name(name), thing_key(name), create_thing(name, desc), remove_thing.

Also owns the pipeline cache (deduplicates pipeline creation), pipeline signature and render-state signature caches (16-bit ids used in the drawer's sort key), and the material slot allocator.

Le registre central de ressources côté CPU. Possède sept sortes de ressources, indexées par des clés de slot-map stables : TextureKey, GeometryKey, ShaderKey, PipelineKey, MaterialKey, MeshKey, BufferKey. Chaque sorte expose le même pattern d'accès : thing(key) (chemin rapide), thing_by_name(name), thing_key(name), create_thing(name, desc), remove_thing.

Possède aussi le cache de pipelines (déduplique la création de pipelines), les caches de signature de pipeline et de signature d'état de rendu (ids 16 bits utilisés dans la sort key du drawer), et l'allocateur de slots de matériaux.

Example

Exemple

let rm_arc = Engine::resource_manager()?;
let mut rm = rm_arc.lock().unwrap();
let mesh_key = rm.create_mesh("player".to_string(), mesh_desc)?;
let mat = rm.material_by_name("steel");
method

Add a mesh to an existing geometry resource
Add a mesh to an existing geometry resource

Add a single LOD variant to an existing submesh.
threshold is the (drop, raise) pair for the new frontier; pass None only when adding the very first LOD of the submesh.
Add a single LOD variant to an existing submesh.
threshold is the (drop, raise) pair for the new frontier; pass None only when adding the very first LOD of the submesh.

method

ResourceManager::create_default_frame_uniform_buffer

pub fn create_default_frame_uniform_buffer( &mut self, name: String, graphics_device: Arc<Mutex<dyn graphics_device::GraphicsDevice>>, ) -> Result<BufferKey>

Convenience constructor for the engine's canonical frame uniform buffer layout (16 fields: view, projection, viewProjection, cameraPosition, cameraDirection, sunDirection, sunColor, ambientColor, time, deltaTime, frameIndex, exposure, gamma, nearPlane, farPlane, ambientIntensity). Returns a BufferKey; the buffer is a UBO with std140 layout.

Hand the buffer to DefaultUpdater::update_frame every frame; fields not touched by the updater (sun, time, exposure…) keep their factory defaults until you write them yourself.

Constructeur de commodité pour la disposition canonique du frame uniform buffer du moteur (16 champs : view, projection, viewProjection, cameraPosition, cameraDirection, sunDirection, sunColor, ambientColor, time, deltaTime, frameIndex, exposure, gamma, nearPlane, farPlane, ambientIntensity). Retourne un BufferKey ; le buffer est un UBO en disposition std140.

Passez le buffer à DefaultUpdater::update_frame chaque frame ; les champs non touchés par l'updater (sun, time, exposure…) gardent leurs valeurs par défaut d'usine jusqu'à ce que vous les écriviez vous-même.

Example

Exemple

let frame_key = rm.create_default_frame_uniform_buffer("frame".to_string(), gd_arc)?;
let frame_buf = rm.buffer(frame_key).unwrap().clone();
updater.update_frame(&camera, &frame_buf)?;
method

ResourceManager::create_default_instance_buffer

pub fn create_default_instance_buffer( &mut self, name: String, graphics_device: Arc<Mutex<dyn graphics_device::GraphicsDevice>>, count: u32, ) -> Result<BufferKey>

Convenience constructor for the engine's canonical per-instance SSBO layout (9 fields: world, previousWorld, inverseWorld, materialSlotId, flags, lightCount, customData, lightIndices0, lightIndices1). The count argument sets the number of slots — must be at least scene.draw_slot_high_water_mark() at any time.

Constructeur de commodité pour la disposition canonique du SSBO par instance du moteur (9 champs : world, previousWorld, inverseWorld, materialSlotId, flags, lightCount, customData, lightIndices0, lightIndices1). L'argument count fixe le nombre de slots — doit valoir au moins scene.draw_slot_high_water_mark() à tout moment.

Example

Exemple

let inst_key = rm.create_default_instance_buffer("instances".to_string(), gd_arc, /* count */ 4096)?;
method

ResourceManager::create_default_light_buffer

pub fn create_default_light_buffer( &mut self, name: String, graphics_device: Arc<Mutex<dyn graphics_device::GraphicsDevice>>, count: u32, ) -> Result<BufferKey>

Convenience constructor for the engine's canonical light SSBO layout: 5 Vec4 fields per light (positionType, directionRange, colorIntensity, spotParams, attenuation). Slot index = light.light_slot().

Constructeur de commodité pour la disposition canonique du SSBO de lumières du moteur : 5 champs Vec4 par lumière (positionType, directionRange, colorIntensity, spotParams, attenuation). Index de slot = light.light_slot().

Example

Exemple

let light_key = rm.create_default_light_buffer("lights".to_string(), gd_arc, /* count */ 128)?;
method

ResourceManager::create_default_material_buffer

pub fn create_default_material_buffer( &mut self, name: String, graphics_device: Arc<Mutex<dyn graphics_device::GraphicsDevice>>, count: u32, ) -> Result<BufferKey>

Convenience constructor for the engine's canonical material SSBO layout — PBR base parameters plus 5 texture slots (each represented as 3 fields: {name}Texture u32 bindless index, {name}Sampler u32 sampler index, {name}Layer u32 layer). Slot index = material.slot_id().

Constructeur de commodité pour la disposition canonique du SSBO de matériaux du moteur — paramètres PBR de base plus 5 slots de texture (chacun représenté par 3 champs : {name}Texture u32 index bindless, {name}Sampler u32 index de sampler, {name}Layer u32 couche). Index de slot = material.slot_id().

Example

Exemple

let mat_key = rm.create_default_material_buffer("materials".to_string(), gd_arc, /* count */ 256)?;
rm.sync_materials_to_buffer(rm.buffer(mat_key).unwrap())?;
method

ResourceManager::resolve_pipeline

pub fn resolve_pipeline( &mut self, vertex_shader: ShaderKey, fragment_shader: ShaderKey, vertex_layout: Arc<graphics_device::VertexLayout>, topology: graphics_device::PrimitiveTopology, color_blend: &graphics_device::ColorBlendState, polygon_mode: graphics_device::PolygonMode, pass_info: &PassInfo, graphics_device: &mut dyn graphics_device::GraphicsDevice, ) -> Result<PipelineKey>

The pipeline-cache entry point. Hashes the (vertex_shader, fragment_shader, vertex_layout, topology, color_blend, polygon_mode, color_formats, depth_format, sample_count) tuple; on hit returns the cached PipelineKey; on miss compiles a new pipeline via gd.create_pipeline and caches it under an auto-generated name.

Drawers always go through this — direct create_pipeline calls are reserved for fullscreen passes that you want to keep out of the cache.

Le point d'entrée du cache de pipelines. Hashe le tuple (vertex_shader, fragment_shader, vertex_layout, topology, color_blend, polygon_mode, color_formats, depth_format, sample_count) ; en cas de hit, retourne le PipelineKey caché ; en cas de miss, compile un nouveau pipeline via gd.create_pipeline et le cache sous un nom auto-généré.

Les drawers passent toujours par cette méthode — les appels directs à create_pipeline sont réservés aux passes fullscreen que vous voulez garder hors du cache.

Example

Exemple

let pk = rm.resolve_pipeline(
    vs_key, fs_key, vertex_layout_arc.clone(),
    PrimitiveTopology::TriangleList,
    &ColorBlendState::default(),
    PolygonMode::Fill,
    &pass_info,
    &mut *gd,
)?;
method

ResourceManager::sync_materials_to_buffer

pub fn sync_materials_to_buffer(&self, buffer: &Buffer) -> Result<()>
Sync all material parameters into a GPU buffer.
For each material, matches params by name against buffer fields. Copies values only when name AND type match. Non-blocking warnings for mismatches (the function never fails on a mismatch).
Sync all material parameters into a GPU buffer.
For each material, matches params by name against buffer fields. Copies values only when name AND type match. Non-blocking warnings for mismatches (the function never fails on a mismatch).

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.sync_materials_to_buffer(/* args */)?;
method

Texture

pub struct Texture

Resource-level texture. Wraps an Arc<dyn graphics_device::Texture> plus optional atlas region metadata: per-layer named regions (x, y, width, height) queryable via region_id(layer_name, region_name) for sub-rect addressing in shaders.

Texture au niveau ressource. Encapsule un Arc<dyn graphics_device::Texture> plus des métadonnées optionnelles d'atlas : régions nommées par couche (x, y, width, height) interrogeables via region_id(nom_couche, nom_region) pour un adressage de sous-rectangle dans les shaders.

Example

Exemple

let tex_key = rm.create_texture("ui_atlas".to_string(), TextureDesc {
    graphics_device: gd_arc.clone(),
    texture: graphics_device::TextureDesc { /* ... */ },
    layers: vec![LayerDesc {
        name: "main".into(), layer_index: 0, data: None,
        regions: vec![
            AtlasRegionDesc { name: "button".into(), region: AtlasRegion { x: 0, y: 0, width: 64, height: 32 } },
        ],
    }],
})?;
method

Texture::graphics_device_texture

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::culler

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::culler

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/scene/culler.rs
struct

BruteForceCuller

pub struct BruteForceCuller

Culler that returns every instance with its view-space depth — no actual culling. Useful as a baseline for measurement, for debugging, or for tiny scenes where the frustum test costs more than the saved draws.

Culler qui retourne toutes les instances avec leur profondeur en espace view — aucun culling effectif. Utile comme baseline de mesure, pour le débogage, ou pour de toutes petites scènes où le test de frustum coûte plus que les draws épargnés.

Example

Exemple

let mut culler = BruteForceCuller::new();
culler.cull_into(&scene, &camera, None, &mut visible);
method

BruteForceCuller::new

pub fn new() -> Self
Create a new BruteForceCuller.
Crée un nouveau BruteForceCuller.

Example

Exemple

let result = BruteForceCuller::new(/* args */);
struct

FrustumCuller

pub struct FrustumCuller

Frustum-based culler. Without a SceneIndex, walks every instance and tests its world-space AABB against the frustum (O(n)). With one, delegates to idx.query_frustum(...) for hierarchical acceleration (O(log n) for an octree on uniformly distributed data).

Culler basé sur le frustum. Sans SceneIndex, parcourt chaque instance et teste son AABB en espace world contre le frustum (O(n)). Avec un index, délègue à idx.query_frustum(...) pour une accélération hiérarchique (O(log n) pour un octree sur données uniformément distribuées).

Example

Exemple

let mut culler = FrustumCuller::new();
culler.cull_into(&scene, &camera, Some(&octree), &mut visible);
method

FrustumCuller::new

pub fn new() -> Self
Create a new FrustumCuller.
Crée un nouveau FrustumCuller.

Example

Exemple

let result = FrustumCuller::new(/* args */);
trait

CameraCuller

pub trait CameraCuller: Send + Sync

Trait abstracting culling strategies. The engine ships two implementations: BruteForceCuller (returns all instances with their depth — useful as a baseline) and FrustumCuller (frustum-tests every instance, using a SceneIndex when supplied for hierarchical acceleration).

Trait abstrayant les stratégies de culling. Le moteur fournit deux implémentations : BruteForceCuller (retourne toutes les instances avec leur profondeur — utile comme baseline) et FrustumCuller (teste le frustum sur chaque instance, en utilisant un SceneIndex s'il est fourni pour une accélération hiérarchique).

Example

Exemple

let mut culler = FrustumCuller::new();
let mut visible = VisibleInstances::new_empty();
culler.cull_into(&scene, &camera, Some(&octree), &mut visible);

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::drawer

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::drawer

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/scene/drawer.rs
struct

ForwardDrawer

pub struct ForwardDrawer

Forward-rendering drawer. Three phases:

  1. Fill — per VisibleSubMesh, validate the cached pipeline (RenderSubMeshPass::is_pipeline_valid) or call ResourceManager::resolve_pipeline to compile a fresh one; build the DrawCall + 64-bit sort key (signature_id | pipeline_sort_id | geometry_sort_id | render_state_sig).
  2. Sort — radix sort the queue by sort key (rdst crate, unstable LSD).
  3. Emit — iterate sorted draws, rebind pipeline / textures / per-pass set / geometry / dynamic state only when the corresponding component changes; push the draw slot as a push constant; call draw_indexed or draw.

Default capacity 4096 draw calls. Capacity grows once on demand, then steady-state has zero allocation.

Drawer de rendu en forward. Trois phases :

  1. Remplissage — pour chaque VisibleSubMesh, valide le pipeline caché (RenderSubMeshPass::is_pipeline_valid) ou appelle ResourceManager::resolve_pipeline pour en compiler un frais ; construit le DrawCall et la clé de tri 64 bits (signature_id | pipeline_sort_id | geometry_sort_id | render_state_sig).
  2. Tri — radix sort de la file par clé de tri (crate rdst, instable LSD).
  3. Émission — itère les draws triés, rebind pipeline / textures / set par-pass / géométrie / état dynamique uniquement quand le composant correspondant change ; pousse le draw slot en push constant ; appelle draw_indexed ou draw.

Capacité par défaut 4096 draw calls. La capacité croît une fois à la demande, puis le régime stationnaire est sans allocation.

Example

Exemple

let mut drawer = ForwardDrawer::with_capacity(8192);
drawer.draw(&mut scene, &view, &mut *cmd, &pass_info, &binding_group, /* bind_textures */ true)?;
method

ForwardDrawer::new

pub fn new() -> Self
Create a ForwardDrawer with the default preallocated draw-call capacity.
Create a ForwardDrawer with the default preallocated draw-call capacity.

Example

Exemple

let result = ForwardDrawer::new(/* args */);
method

ForwardDrawer::with_capacity

pub fn with_capacity(capacity: usize) -> Self
Create a ForwardDrawer with a specific preallocated capacity.
Create a ForwardDrawer with a specific preallocated capacity.

Example

Exemple

let result = ForwardDrawer::with_capacity(/* args */);
trait

Drawer

pub trait Drawer: Send + Sync

Trait for draw strategies. draw(scene, view, cmd, pass_info, binding_group, bind_textures) records draw commands for a RenderView into a command list. The bind_textures: bool gate lets shadow passes skip the bindless set 0 bind.

The engine ships ForwardDrawer as the only built-in implementation; you can plug in your own (deferred, GPU-driven, …).

Trait pour les stratégies de draw. draw(scene, view, cmd, pass_info, binding_group, bind_textures) enregistre les commandes de draw pour un RenderView dans une command list. Le booléen bind_textures permet aux passes de shadow de sauter le bind du set 0 bindless.

Le moteur fournit ForwardDrawer comme seule implémentation intégrée ; vous pouvez brancher la vôtre (deferred, GPU-driven…).

Example

Exemple

let mut drawer: Box<dyn Drawer> = Box::new(ForwardDrawer::new());
drawer.draw(&mut scene, &view, &mut *cmd, &pass_info, &binding_group, true)?;

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::light

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::light

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/scene/light.rs
struct

Light

pub struct Light

A point or spot light in the scene. Carries position, direction (for spots), color, intensity, range, attenuation factors, optional spot inner/outer angles, and an enabled flag. The directional sun is not a Light — it lives in the frame uniform's sunDirection / sunColor.

Modify lights through Scene::set_light_*; the dirty-transforms vs dirty-data sets distinguish spatial changes (position / direction / range / type) from visual changes (color / intensity / attenuation / spot angles / enabled).

Une lumière ponctuelle ou spot dans la scène. Porte position, direction (pour les spots), couleur, intensité, range, facteurs d'atténuation, angles intérieur/extérieur optionnels du spot, et un drapeau enabled. Le soleil directionnel n'est pas un Light — il vit dans le frame uniform sunDirection / sunColor.

Modifiez les lumières via Scene::set_light_* ; les sets dirty-transforms vs dirty-data distinguent les changements spatiaux (position / direction / range / type) des changements visuels (couleur / intensité / atténuation / angles spot / enabled).

Example

Exemple

scene.set_light_position(lk, Vec3::new(1.0, 5.0, 2.0));
scene.set_light_color(lk, Vec3::new(1.0, 0.5, 0.2));
scene.set_light_intensity(lk, 100.0);

LightKey

Stable key for a Light within a Scene.
Keys remain valid even after other lights are removed. A key becomes invalid only when its own light is removed.

Keys remain valid even after other lights are removed. A key becomes invalid only when its own light is removed.

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::lod

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::lod

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/scene/lod.rs
fn

apply_hysteresis

pub fn apply_hysteresis( current: u8, screen_size: f32, thresholds: &[(f32, f32)], ) -> u8
LOD hysteresis selection.
Industry-standard two-threshold hysteresis (Unity LOD Group, Unreal Dithered LOD Transitions): each frontier between two consecutive LODs has a (drop, raise) pair with raise > drop. Between the two values, whichever LOD is currently bound stays bound — that's the "dead zone" that absorbs small frame-to-frame jitter of the screen-space metric.
Direct jump of several LODs in a single frame is allowed when the screen-size metric changes abruptly (camera cut, teleport, spawn). Residual popping is meant to be masked by dither + TAA at the shading stage, not by throttling the LOD selection temporally. Select the new LOD index from the previous one, the current screen-space size of the object, and the per-frontier thresholds.

Arguments


  • current — LOD index bound last frame.
  • screen_size — projected diameter in pixels.
  • thresholds — (drop, raise) pairs, one per frontier.
thresholds.len() must equal lod_count - 1. Must be decreasing: thresholds[i].0 > thresholds[i+1].1.

Semantics


  • **Drop to a coarser LOD** (larger index): happens as soon as the target
LOD, computed directly from screen_size against the drop thresholds, is >= current. The jump can span several LODs.
  • **Rise to a finer LOD** (smaller index): one frontier at a time, and
only if screen_size exceeds that frontier's raise threshold.
  • The result is clamped to [0, thresholds.len()] (i.e. a valid LOD
index even when current is out of range of the current chain).
LOD hysteresis selection.
Industry-standard two-threshold hysteresis (Unity LOD Group, Unreal Dithered LOD Transitions): each frontier between two consecutive LODs has a (drop, raise) pair with raise > drop. Between the two values, whichever LOD is currently bound stays bound — that's the "dead zone" that absorbs small frame-to-frame jitter of the screen-space metric.
Direct jump of several LODs in a single frame is allowed when the screen-size metric changes abruptly (camera cut, teleport, spawn). Residual popping is meant to be masked by dither + TAA at the shading stage, not by throttling the LOD selection temporally. Select the new LOD index from the previous one, the current screen-space size of the object, and the per-frontier thresholds.

Arguments


  • current — LOD index bound last frame.
  • screen_size — projected diameter in pixels.
  • thresholds — (drop, raise) pairs, one per frontier.
thresholds.len() must equal lod_count - 1. Must be decreasing: thresholds[i].0 > thresholds[i+1].1.

Semantics


  • **Drop to a coarser LOD** (larger index): happens as soon as the target
LOD, computed directly from screen_size against the drop thresholds, is >= current. The jump can span several LODs.
  • **Rise to a finer LOD** (smaller index): one frontier at a time, and
only if screen_size exceeds that frontier's raise threshold.
  • The result is clamped to [0, thresholds.len()] (i.e. a valid LOD
index even when current is out of range of the current chain).

Example

Exemple

let result = apply_hysteresis(/* args */);

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::octree_scene_index

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::octree_scene_index

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/scene/octree_scene_index.rs
struct

OctreeSceneIndex

pub struct OctreeSceneIndex

An octree-based spatial index using single-node placement: each instance is stored in the deepest node whose AABB fully contains it (no duplication, no result-deduplication HashSet needed). Tradeoff: instances straddling a child boundary stay in the parent.

Construct with the world bounding box and a desired depth; total node count is (8^(D+1) − 1) / 7. At depth 4 that's 6553 preallocated nodes.

Index spatial basé sur un octree avec placement à nœud unique : chaque instance est stockée dans le nœud le plus profond dont l'AABB la contient entièrement (pas de duplication, pas de HashSet de déduplication). Compromis : les instances chevauchant une frontière de fils restent dans le parent.

Construit avec la boîte englobante du monde et une profondeur souhaitée ; le nombre total de nœuds est (8^(D+1) − 1) / 7. À la profondeur 4, ça fait 6553 nœuds pré-alloués.

Example

Exemple

let world = AABB { min: Vec3::splat(-1000.0), max: Vec3::splat(1000.0) };
let octree = OctreeSceneIndex::new(world, /* depth */ 5);
method

OctreeSceneIndex::new

pub fn new(world_aabb: AABB, max_depth: u32) -> Self
Create a new static octree with the given world bounds and depth.

Arguments


  • world_aabb - The world-space AABB encompassing the entire scene
  • max_depth - Maximum tree depth (root = 0). Total nodes = (8^(d+1) - 1) / 7.
Typical values: 4–6 for most scenes.
Create a new static octree with the given world bounds and depth.

Arguments


  • world_aabb - The world-space AABB encompassing the entire scene
  • max_depth - Maximum tree depth (root = 0). Total nodes = (8^(d+1) - 1) / 7.
Typical values: 4–6 for most scenes.

Example

Exemple

let result = OctreeSceneIndex::new(/* args */);

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::render_instance

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::render_instance

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/scene/render_instance.rs
struct

AABB

pub struct AABB

Axis-Aligned Bounding Box in local or world space, depending on context. Four operations: transformed(matrix) (Arvo's exact method, no corner enumeration), contains, intersects, center, closest_point.

Boîte englobante alignée aux axes en espace local ou monde, selon le contexte. Quatre opérations : transformed(matrix) (méthode exacte d'Arvo, sans énumération des coins), contains, intersects, center, closest_point.

Example

Exemple

let local_aabb = AABB { min: Vec3::splat(-1.0), max: Vec3::splat(1.0) };
let world_aabb = local_aabb.transformed(&instance.world_matrix);
if world_aabb.intersects(&query_box) { /* ... */ }
field

AABB::max

pub max: Vec3
Maximum corner (x, y, z)
Maximum corner (x, y, z)

Example

Exemple

let value = instance.max;
field

AABB::min

pub min: Vec3
Minimum corner (x, y, z)
Minimum corner (x, y, z)

Example

Exemple

let value = instance.min;
method

AABB::center

pub fn center(&self) -> Vec3
Get the center of the AABB
Get the center of the AABB

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.center(/* args */);
method

AABB::closest_point

AABB::transformed

pub fn transformed(&self, matrix: &Mat4) -> AABB
Transform this local-space AABB by a matrix, returning a new AABB.
Uses the Arvo method: projects each matrix axis onto the AABB extents for an exact (tight) result without transforming all 8 corners.
Transform this local-space AABB by a matrix, returning a new AABB.
Uses the Arvo method: projects each matrix axis onto the AABB extents for an exact (tight) result without transforming all 8 corners.

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.transformed(/* args */);
struct

RenderInstance

pub struct RenderInstance

The in-scene representation of a renderable entity. References a Geometry (by key + mesh id) — does not duplicate GPU data — plus a world matrix, a flag bitfield (FLAG_VISIBLE / FLAG_CAST_SHADOW / FLAG_RECEIVE_SHADOW), a local-space AABB, and a Vec<RenderSubMesh> with one entry per submesh of the referenced mesh.

Each RenderSubMesh carries its draw slot, a 64-bit pass mask, a [u8; 64] pass-type-to-index lookup, and a compact list of RenderSubMeshPasses with cached pipeline keys per pass.

La représentation in-scene d'une entité renderable. Référence une Geometry (par clé + id de mesh) — ne duplique pas les données GPU — plus une matrice world, un bitfield de flags (FLAG_VISIBLE / FLAG_CAST_SHADOW / FLAG_RECEIVE_SHADOW), une AABB en espace local, et un Vec<RenderSubMesh> avec une entrée par submesh du mesh référencé.

Chaque RenderSubMesh porte son draw slot, un pass mask 64 bits, une lookup [u8; 64] pass-type vers index, et une liste compacte de RenderSubMeshPass avec des clés de pipeline cachées par passe.

Example

Exemple

let inst = scene.render_instance(key).unwrap();
let sm = inst.sub_mesh(0).unwrap();
if sm.is_pass_active(PASS_OPAQUE) {
    // submesh participates in the opaque pass
}
method

RenderInstanceKey

pub struct RenderInstanceKey
Stable key for a RenderInstance within a Scene.
Keys remain valid even after other instances are removed. A key becomes invalid only when its own instance is removed.
Stable key for a RenderInstance within a Scene.
Keys remain valid even after other instances are removed. A key becomes invalid only when its own instance is removed.

struct

RenderSubMesh

pub struct RenderSubMesh
A single drawable submesh of a RenderInstance.
Each submesh has its own material and a set of rendering passes derived from that material's MaterialPass list. The pass_mask bitmask controls which passes are active for drawing (mutable at runtime), while the pass_type_to_index table provides O(1) lookup from a pass_type to the compact passes vec.
A single drawable submesh of a RenderInstance.
Each submesh has its own material and a set of rendering passes derived from that material's MaterialPass list. The pass_mask bitmask controls which passes are active for drawing (mutable at runtime), while the pass_type_to_index table provides O(1) lookup from a pass_type to the compact passes vec.

struct

RenderSubMeshPass

pub struct RenderSubMeshPass
Per-pass pipeline data for a RenderSubMesh.
Each entry corresponds to one rendering pass of the submesh. The material and material pass index are stored per-pass so that different passes of the same submesh can reference different materials (e.g. a PBR material for forward rendering and a simpler material for shadow casting).
Per-pass pipeline data for a RenderSubMesh.
Each entry corresponds to one rendering pass of the submesh. The material and material pass index are stored per-pass so that different passes of the same submesh can reference different materials (e.g. a PBR material for forward rendering and a simpler material for shadow casting).

Example

Exemple

// Use the type in a function signature or storage:
let x: RenderSubMeshPass;

RenderQueue

pub struct RenderQueue
Preallocated queue of draw calls + sort entries.
Usage pattern per frame (no allocation): ``ignore queue.clear(); for visible in ... { queue.push(dc, sort_key); } queue.sort(); for dc in queue.iter_sorted() { /* emit */ } ``
Preallocated queue of draw calls + sort entries.
Usage pattern per frame (no allocation): ``ignore queue.clear(); for visible in ... { queue.push(dc, sort_key); } queue.sort(); for dc in queue.iter_sorted() { /* emit */ } ``

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::render_view

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::render_view

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/scene/render_view.rs
struct

RenderView

pub struct RenderView

Per-pass draw list produced by the ViewDispatcher, consumed by a Drawer. Carries a camera snapshot, a pass_type: u8, and a Vec<VisibleSubMesh> with each draw item's resolved (submesh_index, pass_index, lod_index). Reused frame-to-frame via clear().

Draw-list par passe produite par le ViewDispatcher, consommée par un Drawer. Contient un instantané de la caméra, un pass_type: u8, et un Vec<VisibleSubMesh> avec les (submesh_index, pass_index, lod_index) résolus de chaque item à dessiner. Réutilisée d'une frame à l'autre via clear().

Example

Exemple

let mut view = RenderView::new(camera.clone(), PASS_OPAQUE);
ViewDispatcher::dispatch(&visible, &mut scene, &rm, std::slice::from_mut(&mut view));
method

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::scene

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::scene

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/scene/scene.rs
struct

Scene

pub struct Scene

The mutable per-frame world. Owns every RenderInstance and Light as SlotMap entries with stable keys, plus six SwapSets for change tracking (instances: dirty / new / removed; lights: dirty-transform / dirty-data / new / removed). Also owns two SlotAllocators for instance draw slots and light SSBO slots.

Mutating helpers (set_world_matrix, set_light_position, …) automatically mark the appropriate dirty set; the DefaultUpdater drains them in three phases (removed → new → dirty) once per frame.

Le monde mutable par frame. Possède chaque RenderInstance et Light comme entrées de SlotMap avec des clés stables, plus six SwapSets pour le suivi des modifications (instances : dirty / new / removed ; lights : dirty-transform / dirty-data / new / removed). Possède également deux SlotAllocators pour les slots de draw d'instances et les slots de SSBO de lumières.

Les helpers de mutation (set_world_matrix, set_light_position…) marquent automatiquement le bon set dirty ; le DefaultUpdater les draine en trois phases (removed → new → dirty) une fois par frame.

Example

Exemple

let sm = Engine::scene_manager()?;
let mut scene = sm.lock().unwrap();
let rm = Engine::resource_manager()?.lock().unwrap();
let inst = scene.create_render_instance(
    mesh_key,
    Mat4::from_translation(Vec3::new(0.0, 1.0, 0.0)),
    aabb,
    vertex_shader_key,
    &[],
    &rm,
)?;
method

Scene::clear

pub fn clear(&mut self)
Remove all render instances, lights, and reset allocators.
Remove all render instances, lights, and reset allocators.

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.clear(/* args */);
method

Scene::create_light

pub fn create_light(&mut self, desc: LightDesc) -> LightKey

Spawn a new Light (point or spot). Allocates a slot from light_slot_allocator, inserts into the slot map, marks new_lights. The next update_lights call writes all five Vec4 fields of the light SSBO.

Crée une nouvelle Light (point ou spot). Alloue un slot depuis light_slot_allocator, l'insère dans le slot map, marque new_lights. Le prochain appel à update_lights écrira les cinq champs Vec4 du SSBO de lumières.

Example

Exemple

let lk = scene.create_light(LightDesc::Point {
    position: Vec3::new(0.0, 5.0, 0.0),
    color: Vec3::ONE,
    intensity: 50.0,
    range: 20.0,
    attenuation_constant: 0.0,
    attenuation_linear: 0.0,
    attenuation_quadratic: 1.0,
});
method

Scene::create_render_instance

pub fn create_render_instance( &mut self, mesh_key: MeshKey, world_matrix: Mat4, bounding_box: AABB, vertex_shader: ShaderKey, vertex_shader_overrides: &[VertexShaderOverride], resource_manager: &ResourceManager, ) -> Result<RenderInstanceKey>

Spawn a new render instance. Allocates one draw slot per submesh from Scene::draw_slot_allocator, builds a RenderInstance from the referenced Mesh, inserts it into the slot map, marks new_instances. The next Updater::update_instances call writes its GPU fields and inserts it into the spatial index.

Crée une nouvelle render instance. Alloue un draw slot par submesh depuis Scene::draw_slot_allocator, construit une RenderInstance depuis le Mesh référencé, l'insère dans le slot map, marque new_instances. Le prochain appel à Updater::update_instances écrira ses champs GPU et l'insérera dans l'index spatial.

Example

Exemple

let key = scene.create_render_instance(mesh_key, Mat4::IDENTITY, aabb, vs_key, &[], &rm)?;
method

Scene::remove_render_instance

pub fn remove_render_instance(&mut self, key: RenderInstanceKey) -> bool

Mark an instance for removal. The instance stays in the slot map until removed_instances() is called (typically in the next update_instances), at which point its draw slots are returned to the allocator and the instance is dropped from the slot map. Returns false on invalid key.

Marque une instance pour suppression. L'instance reste dans le slot map jusqu'à ce que removed_instances() soit appelé (typiquement au prochain update_instances), moment où ses draw slots sont rendus à l'allocateur et l'instance est retirée du slot map. Retourne false en cas de clé invalide.

Example

Exemple

scene.remove_render_instance(key);
method

Scene::set_world_matrix

pub fn set_world_matrix(&mut self, key: RenderInstanceKey, matrix: Mat4) -> bool

Update an instance's world matrix and mark it as dirty. The next update_instances call will rewrite the GPU fields (world / previousWorld / inverseWorld) and update the spatial index. Returns false if the key is invalid.

Met à jour la matrice world d'une instance et la marque dirty. Le prochain appel à update_instances réécrira les champs GPU (world / previousWorld / inverseWorld) et mettra à jour l'index spatial. Retourne false si la clé est invalide.

Example

Exemple

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::scene_index

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::scene_index

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/scene/scene_index.rs
trait

SceneIndex

pub trait SceneIndex: Send + Sync

Trait for spatial-acceleration structures (octree, BVH, k-d tree). Implementors expose insert / update / remove for instance lifecycle and query_frustum for fast frustum culling. The engine ships OctreeSceneIndex as the default implementation; you can write your own and pass it to the culler.

Trait pour les structures d'accélération spatiale (octree, BVH, k-d tree). Les implémentations exposent insert / update / remove pour le cycle de vie des instances et query_frustum pour le culling rapide. Le moteur fournit OctreeSceneIndex comme implémentation par défaut ; vous pouvez écrire la vôtre et la transmettre au culler.

Example

Exemple

let mut index = OctreeSceneIndex::new(world_bounds, /* max_depth */ 4);
culler.cull_into(&scene, &camera, Some(&index), &mut visible);

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::scene_manager

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/scene/scene_manager.rs
struct

SceneManager

pub struct SceneManager

Singleton wrapper that holds the global Scene. Created via Engine::create_scene_manager, retrieved via Engine::scene_manager. Today it wraps a single scene; future versions may add multi-scene transitions.

Wrapper singleton qui détient la Scene globale. Créé via Engine::create_scene_manager, récupéré via Engine::scene_manager. Aujourd'hui il encapsule une seule scène ; de futures versions pourraient ajouter des transitions multi-scènes.

Example

Exemple

let sm = Engine::scene_manager()?.lock().unwrap();
let scene = sm.scene();

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::updater

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::updater

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/scene/updater.rs
struct

DefaultUpdater

pub struct DefaultUpdater

Production updater implementation. update_instances processes the three SwapSets in order: removed (drains and frees draw slots, removes from spatial index) → new (writes all GPU fields and inserts into spatial index, requires the ResourceManager to look up material slot ids) → dirty (writes world / previousWorld / inverseWorld and updates the spatial index). assign_lights performs sphere-AABB and cone-AABB tests to score lights, takes the top 8 per instance, packs into lightIndices0 / lightIndices1.

Persistent buffers (enabled_light_keys, candidates) are reused across frames — zero allocation in steady state.

Implémentation d'updater de production. update_instances traite les trois SwapSets dans l'ordre : removed (draine et libère les draw slots, retire de l'index spatial) → new (écrit tous les champs GPU et insère dans l'index spatial, nécessite le ResourceManager pour retrouver les slot ids des matériaux) → dirty (écrit world / previousWorld / inverseWorld et met à jour l'index spatial). assign_lights effectue des tests sphère-AABB et cône-AABB pour scorer les lumières, garde les 8 meilleures par instance, les empile dans lightIndices0 / lightIndices1.

Les buffers persistants (enabled_light_keys, candidates) sont réutilisés entre frames — zéro allocation en régime stationnaire.

Example

Exemple

let mut updater = DefaultUpdater::new();
// inside frame loop:
updater.update_frame(&camera, &frame_buf)?;
updater.update_instances(&mut scene, Some(&mut octree), &instance_buf)?;
method

DefaultUpdater::new

pub fn new() -> Self
Create a new DefaultUpdater.
Crée un nouveau DefaultUpdater.

Example

Exemple

let result = DefaultUpdater::new(/* args */);
struct

NoOpUpdater

pub struct NoOpUpdater
No-op updater — does nothing.
Placeholder for scenes that don't need GPU buffer synchronization.
No-op updater — does nothing.
Placeholder for scenes that don't need GPU buffer synchronization.

Example

Exemple

// Use the type in a function signature or storage:
let x: NoOpUpdater;
method

NoOpUpdater::new

pub fn new() -> Self
Create a new NoOpUpdater.
Crée un nouveau NoOpUpdater.

Example

Exemple

let result = NoOpUpdater::new(/* args */);
trait

Updater

pub trait Updater: Send + Sync

Trait for frame-state synchronization between CPU and GPU. The four methods (update_frame, update_instances, update_lights, assign_lights) are called once per frame in this order. The engine ships NoOpUpdater (every method is Ok(())) and DefaultUpdater (production implementation).

Trait pour la synchronisation d'état par frame entre CPU et GPU. Les quatre méthodes (update_frame, update_instances, update_lights, assign_lights) sont appelées une fois par frame dans cet ordre. Le moteur fournit NoOpUpdater (chaque méthode retourne Ok(())) et DefaultUpdater (implémentation de production).

Example

Exemple

let mut updater = DefaultUpdater::new();
updater.update_frame(&camera, &frame_buf)?;
updater.update_instances(&mut scene, Some(&mut octree), &instance_buf)?;
updater.update_lights(&mut scene, &light_buf)?;
updater.assign_lights(&scene, &visible, &instance_buf)?;

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::view_dispatcher

galaxy_3d_engine::scene::view_dispatcher

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/scene/view_dispatcher.rs
struct

ViewDispatcher

pub struct ViewDispatcher

Stateless conversion of a VisibleInstances + Scene + ResourceManager into one or more RenderViews — one per pass-type the frame wants to render. Resolves per-submesh LOD via scene::lod::apply_hysteresis, prunes submeshes whose chosen LOD has zero geometry (intentional way to hide a submesh past a distance).

Conversion sans état d'un VisibleInstances + Scene + ResourceManager en un ou plusieurs RenderViews — un par pass-type que la frame veut rendre. Résout le LOD par submesh via scene::lod::apply_hysteresis, élimine les submeshes dont le LOD choisi n'a aucune géométrie (façon intentionnelle de cacher un submesh au-delà d'une distance).

Example

Exemple

let mut views = [
    RenderView::new(camera.clone(), PASS_OPAQUE),
    RenderView::new(camera.clone(), PASS_SHADOW),
];
ViewDispatcher::dispatch(&visible, &mut scene, &rm, &mut views);
method

ViewDispatcher::dispatch

pub fn dispatch( visible: &VisibleInstances, scene: &mut Scene, rm: &ResourceManager, render_views: &mut [RenderView], )
Dispatch all visible instances from visible into the provided render_views. Each RenderView is cleared then refilled.
render_views must be pre-created with the desired pass_type values. The camera snapshot is copied from visible into each RenderView.
Dispatch all visible instances from visible into the provided render_views. Each RenderView is cleared then refilled.
render_views must be pre-created with the desired pass_type values. The camera snapshot is copied from visible into each RenderView.

Example

Exemple

let result = ViewDispatcher::dispatch(/* args */);
method

ViewDispatcher::new

pub fn new() -> Self
Create a new ViewDispatcher.
Crée un nouveau ViewDispatcher.

Example

Exemple

let result = ViewDispatcher::new(/* args */);

galaxy_3d_engine::utils

galaxy_3d_engine::utils

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/utils/mod.rs
use

slot_allocator

pub use slot_allocator::SlotAllocator
Public re-export.
Réexport public.

Example

Exemple

// Re-exported: use the symbol as if it lived in this module.
use

SlotAllocator

pub use slot_allocator::SlotAllocator;

Allocator of stable u32 indices for GPU slot tables (material SSBO slots, light SSBO slots, per-instance draw slots, bindless texture indices). Internal state: a free-list (LIFO) and a monotonically increasing next_id counter. Both alloc and free are O(1).

The allocator never grows the underlying GPU buffer — the caller is expected to preallocate at least high_water_mark() slots. Use high_water_mark() to size your SSBO.

Allocateur d'indices u32 stables pour les tables de slots GPU (slots du SSBO de matériaux, slots du SSBO de lumières, slots de draw par instance, indices bindless de textures). État interne : une free-list (LIFO) et un compteur next_id monotone. alloc et free sont en O(1).

L'allocateur ne fait jamais grandir le buffer GPU sous-jacent — c'est à l'appelant de pré-allouer au moins high_water_mark() slots. Utilisez high_water_mark() pour dimensionner votre SSBO.

Example

Exemple

let mut alloc = SlotAllocator::new();
let a = alloc.alloc();   // 0
let b = alloc.alloc();   // 1
alloc.free(a);
let c = alloc.alloc();   // 0 (recycled)
assert_eq!(alloc.high_water_mark(), 2);  // size your SSBO with this

galaxy_3d_engine::utils::slot_allocator

galaxy_3d_engine::utils::slot_allocator

file: galaxy_3d_engine/src/utils/slot_allocator.rs
struct

SlotAllocator

pub struct SlotAllocator
Allocates and recycles unique u32 indices.
Manages a pool of slot indices for GPU buffers (SSBO) or any system that needs stable, reusable integer identifiers. Freed indices are recycled on subsequent allocations.

Example


``ignore let mut alloc = SlotAllocator::new(); let a = alloc.alloc(); // 0 let b = alloc.alloc(); // 1 alloc.free(a); // 0 is now available let c = alloc.alloc(); // 0 (recycled) ``
Allocates and recycles unique u32 indices.
Manages a pool of slot indices for GPU buffers (SSBO) or any system that needs stable, reusable integer identifiers. Freed indices are recycled on subsequent allocations.

Example


``ignore let mut alloc = SlotAllocator::new(); let a = alloc.alloc(); // 0 let b = alloc.alloc(); // 1 alloc.free(a); // 0 is now available let c = alloc.alloc(); // 0 (recycled) ``

Example

Exemple

// Use the type in a function signature or storage:
let x: SlotAllocator;
method

SlotAllocator::alloc

pub fn alloc(&mut self) -> u32

Pop a recycled index from the free-list, or issue a fresh one from next_id. Always returns a valid u32 in [0, high_water_mark).

Récupère un index recyclé depuis la free-list, ou en émet un nouveau depuis next_id. Retourne toujours un u32 valide dans [0, high_water_mark).

Example

Exemple

let id = alloc.alloc();
method

SlotAllocator::free

pub fn free(&mut self, id: u32)

Return an index to the free-list for later reuse. Debug-asserts that id < next_id — release builds skip the check, so don't double-free.

Retourne un index dans la free-list pour réutilisation ultérieure. Vérifie en debug_assert que id < next_id — les builds release sautent la vérification, donc ne libérez pas deux fois le même index.

Example

Exemple

alloc.free(id);
method

SlotAllocator::high_water_mark

pub fn high_water_mark(&self) -> u32

The largest id + 1 ever allocated. Use it as the lower bound on your GPU buffer's element count: any GPU buffer addressed by these slots must have at least this capacity.

Le plus grand id + 1 jamais alloué. Utilisez-le comme borne inférieure pour le nombre d'éléments de votre buffer GPU : tout buffer GPU adressé par ces slots doit avoir au moins cette capacité.

Example

Exemple

let needed = alloc.high_water_mark() as usize;
let ssbo = rm.create_default_instance_buffer("inst", gd, needed.max(64) as u32)?;
method

SlotAllocator::is_empty

pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool
Whether no slots are currently allocated
Whether no slots are currently allocated

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.is_empty(/* args */);
method

SlotAllocator::len

pub fn len(&self) -> u32
Number of currently allocated slots

method

SwapSet<K>::flip

pub fn flip(&self) -> &FxHashSet<K>
Flip the active buffer and return a reference to the previous front.
The returned reference holds all keys inserted since the last flip. If no insert happened since the last flip (idle frame), returns an empty set — stale data is never exposed.
Uses Cell for interior mutability — only flips a u8 index, no unsafe code.
Flip the active buffer and return a reference to the previous front.
The returned reference holds all keys inserted since the last flip. If no insert happened since the last flip (idle frame), returns an empty set — stale data is never exposed.
Uses Cell for interior mutability — only flips a u8 index, no unsafe code.

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.flip(/* args */);
method

SwapSet<K>::insert

pub fn insert(&mut self, key: K) -> bool
Insert a key into the front buffer.
If a flip happened since the last insert, the front is lazily cleared first (preserving heap capacity).
Insert a key into the front buffer.
If a flip happened since the last insert, the front is lazily cleared first (preserving heap capacity).

Example

Exemple

let result = instance.insert(/* args */);
method

Check if the front buffer is logically empty.
Check if the front buffer is logically empty.

galaxy_3d_engine_renderer_vulkan::vulkan

galaxy_3d_engine_renderer_vulkan::vulkan

file: galaxy_3d_engine_renderer_vulkan/src/vulkan.rs
struct

DynamicStateCaps

pub struct DynamicStateCaps
Runtime capabilities for optional dynamic states (EXT_extended_dynamic_state3 / EXT_color_write_enable).
Each flag is queried once at device creation and never changes. The branch predictor learns these constant-runtime values in ~2 iterations, making the resulting if-checks effectively free (~0 cycles).
Runtime capabilities for optional dynamic states (EXT_extended_dynamic_state3 / EXT_color_write_enable).
Each flag is queried once at device creation and never changes. The branch predictor learns these constant-runtime values in ~2 iterations, making the resulting if-checks effectively free (~0 cycles).

Example

Exemple

// Use the type in a function signature or storage:
let x: DynamicStateCaps;

file: galaxy_3d_engine_renderer_vulkan/src/vulkan_binding_group.rs
struct

BindingGroup

pub struct BindingGroup
Vulkan binding group implementation
Wraps a VkDescriptorSet handle. The descriptor set itself is managed by the descriptor pool and will be freed when the pool is destroyed. Immutable after creation — create a new BindingGroup to change resources.
Vulkan binding group implementation
Wraps a VkDescriptorSet handle. The descriptor set itself is managed by the descriptor pool and will be freed when the pool is destroyed. Immutable after creation — create a new BindingGroup to change resources.

let x: BindingGroup;

galaxy_3d_engine_renderer_vulkan::vulkan_context

galaxy_3d_engine_renderer_vulkan::vulkan_context

file: galaxy_3d_engine_renderer_vulkan/src/vulkan_context.rs
struct

GpuContext

pub struct GpuContext
Shared GPU context for all Vulkan resources.
This struct is shared (via Arc) by all GPU resources (textures, buffers, etc.) to avoid duplicating device/allocator/queue references in each resource.
Note: Device and instance destruction is handled by VulkanGraphicsDevice::drop() to avoid issues with drop ordering and callback exceptions on Windows.
Shared GPU context for all Vulkan resources.
This struct is shared (via Arc) by all GPU resources (textures, buffers, etc.) to avoid duplicating device/allocator/queue references in each resource.
Note: Device and instance destruction is handled by VulkanGraphicsDevice::drop() to avoid issues with drop ordering and callback exceptions on Windows.

Example

Exemple

// Use the type in a function signature or storage:
let x: GpuContext;
field

GpuContext::allocator

pub allocator: ManuallyDrop<Arc<Mutex<Allocator>>>
GPU memory allocator (shared, requires mutex for thread safety) Wrapped in ManuallyDrop to ensure it's dropped BEFORE the device is destroyed
GPU memory allocator (shared, requires mutex for thread safety) Wrapped in ManuallyDrop to ensure it's dropped BEFORE the device is destroyed

struct

Pipeline

pub struct Pipeline
Vulkan pipeline implementation
Stores the descriptor set layouts internally (Option B design). The layouts are created from BindingGroupLayoutDesc at pipeline creation time and used by VulkanGraphicsDevice::create_binding_group() to allocate descriptor sets.

Stores the descriptor set layouts internally (Option B design). The layouts are created from BindingGroupLayoutDesc at pipeline creation time and used by VulkanGraphicsDevice::create_binding_group() to allocate descriptor sets.
